companyDescription:
S-RM is a global intelligence and cyber security consultancy. Founded in 2005, we have 400+ practitioners spanning nine international offices, serving clients across all regions and major sectors. 

We support our clients by providing intelligence that informs critical decision-making and strategies, from investments and partnerships through to disputes; by helping organisations build resilience to cyber security threats; and by responding to cyber-attacks and organisational crises. 

Client focus is at the heart of what we do. Our advice is direct, honest and objective. We deliver actionable results for our clients by bringing together the best talent and creating teams designed to address unique problems and complex challenges.

For more information: www.s-rminform.com
companySlogan: Your First Call for Investigations and Cyber Security.
descriptionHtml:
We are looking for a consultant to join our Cyber Incident Response team as an Engagement Lead. A successful cyber response only happens when a group of technical experts works seamlessly with a team leader who can get the best out of them.

Role responsibilities include:

  • Project scoping: you will be a first responder to cyber-attacks, running triage calls with clients to understand the scope of the incident and setting out how S-RM can support.
  • Project management: from that first call to the incident's conclusion, you will be responsible for ensuring the response is managed effectively and efficiently, delivering for all stakeholders while on schedule and within budget. Typical workstreams include:
  • Working with clients to ensure that incidents are contained.
  • Overseeing a team of forensic analysts investigating the incident.
  • Advising strategies for responding to cyber threat actors.
  • Providing crisis management expertise to help clients navigate the incident.
  • Relationship management: you will be responsible for building and developing relationships with the various stakeholders involved in a cyber incident, from the victims themselves to lawyers and insurance professionals. 

We nurture a culture of equality, diversity and inclusion, and we are dedicated to developing a workforce that displays a variety of talents, experiences, and perspectives.

WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

We think candidates with the following skills and experience are likely to succeed as Incident Response Engagement Leads at S-RM.

You will need great consulting skills and a developed understanding of networks and security technologies. We will prioritise candidates with demonstrable digital forensics and incident response ('DFIR') experience, especially those that have worked in an incident handling capacity before. However, we'd encourage candidates with adjacent experience to apply. We believe that, with the right training, anyone that fits the profile below can make an excellent Engagement Lead. We have a proven track record of supporting those from diverse backgrounds to develop into this role.

We're looking for:

  • Great communication skills.
  • A calm and empathetic style when under pressure.
  • A thoughtful approach to solving problems. 
  • An instinct to seek the expertise of others and bring diverse skillsets together. 
  • Excellent client service and advocacy skills in your daily work.

Candidates must have permission to work in the Netherlands by the start of their employment.

OUR BENEFITS

We offer thoughtful rewards and support to help our people achieve a great balance in their professional and personal lives, including:

  • 25 days holiday per year (+1 day for every day of service up to a maximum of 30 days);
  • Life insurance – 4x annual salary;
  • Matching pension contribution up to 6% (up to a maximum of 12% combined), and financial education;
  • Military Leave– to a maximum of 5 years;
  • Hybrid working and flexible working hours.
  • Parental Support:
  • Fertility treatment leave – 5 days of leave per cycle of treatment per year;
  • Maternity leave – 26 weeks of full pay followed by 13 weeks of half pay;
  • Paternity leave – 6 weeks of full pay.
  • Various Health and Medical Benefits including:
  • 100% Company paid private medical, dental and vision insurance for employee, partner and children;
  • Gym discounts for you and your partner;
  • EAP programme for you and your immediate family;
  • Free access to the world-famous mindfulness app Headspace.

THE APPLICATION PROCESS

We want to get to know you, and for you to get to know us, to see if we'd be a good fit. We are responsive and respectful of people's time throughout our hiring process.

A typical application process includes:

  • Initial screening of your application by our recruiting team.
  • An interview to assess your baseline technical skills.
  • An interview to discuss your previous experience, broader competencies, and suitability for the role.

companyDescription:
Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is an IT services, consulting, and business solutions organization that has been partnering with many of the world’s largest businesses in their transformation journeys since its inception in 1968. Our consulting led, innovation-driven services help businesses evolve as perpetually adaptive enterprises, staying ahead in an ever-changing digital landscape. Recognized for excellence in software development, we rapidly apply and scale new technologies, driving long-term value and scalable transformation. Many of these relationships have endured for decades, navigating every technology cycle from mainframes in the 1970s to Artificial Intelligence today.
 
A part of the Tata Group, India’s largest multinational business group, TCS has a highly skilled workforce of over 613,069 consultants in 55 countries and 202 service delivery centers across the world.
 
Caution against fraudulent job offers: TCS doesn’t charge any fee throughout the recruitment process. Refer here: https://www.tcs.com/careers/india/recruitment-fraud-alert
descriptionHtml:
About TCS

Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is a global leader in IT services, digital, and business solutions that partners with its clients to simplify, strengthen, and transform their businesses. We ensure the highest levels of certainty and satisfaction through a deep-set commitment to our clients, comprehensive industry expertise, and a global network of innovation and delivery centers.


TCS operates on a global scale & has over 6,08,985 of the world’s best-trained consultants representing 152 nationalities in 53 countries. TCS established its presence in the Netherlands in 1992 and works with leading Dutch Customers across industry sectors, including several NL20 companies. TCS has been ranked #1 in customer satisfaction, among IT services providers in the Netherlands in a survey by Whitelane Research and is recognized as a Top Employer in the Netherlands, Europe & also Global by the Top Employers Institute


For more information, visit www.tcs.com


About the role:

Technical Competency

  • The Service Now SecOps (Security Incident Response, Vulnerability Response) developer would need to have at least 4-8 years of experience with more than 1 year of experience in Security Incident Response/Vulnerability Response modules.
  • ServiceNow implementation experience in Security operations- Security Incident Response, Vulnerability Response, Threat Intelligence, Configuration Compliance, or modules
  • ServiceNow implementation experience in ITSM modules (Incident, Problem, Change, CMDB & Asset– not limited to) will be an added advantage.
  • Working experience in implementation of catalog items and record producers.
  • Working experience in configuring Security incident response applications and OOB/ custom integration build with SIEM tool/ TI enrichment tools
  • Experience in building email integration for importing suspicious emails as Security incidents (Phishing use case)
  • Working experience in configuring Vulnerability response applications and integration with one of vulnerability scanner tool
  • Build service requests from customer requirements including requests, request items and tasks using workflows-to manage data flows from the customer to the support teams providing the service.
  • Uses scripting tools and Service Now functionality create script to automate rote tasks being done in ServiceNow.
  • Performs integrations and process automation using ServiceNow Orchestration.
  • Load, manipulate and maintain data between ServiceNow and other systems.
  • Thorough understanding of ServiceNow Security incident response and Vulnerable Item processes
  • Performs system and integration testing with sample and live data Monitor health, usage, and overall compliance of the application.
  • Hands-on design, development, and deployment experience with the ServiceNow platform


Behavioral competency

  • Effective communication skills with both non-technical and technical audiences (verbal and written)
  • Excellent Interpersonal skills to work with the project team members collaboratively
  • Participate and provide feedback during planning meetings (i.e., pre-iteration, and retrospectives)


Good-to-Have

  • ITIL V3/V4 foundation certification
  • Sec Ops Certification


Responsibility of / Expectations from the Role

  • ServiceNow implementation experience in Security operations- Security Incident Response, Vulnerability Response, Threat Intelligence, Configuration Compliance, or modules
  • ServiceNow implementation experience in ITSM modules (Incident, Problem, Change, CMDB & Asset– not limited to) will be an added advantage.
  • Working experience in implementation of catalog items and record producers.
  • Working experience in configuring Security incident response applications and OOB/ custom integration build with SIEM tool/ TI enrichment tools
  • Experience in building email integration for importing suspicious emails as Security incidents (Phishing use case)
  • Working experience in configuring Vulnerability response applications and integration with one of vulnerability scanner tool
  • Build service requests from customer requirements including requests, request items and tasks using workflows-to manage data flows from the customer to the support teams providing the service.
  • Uses scripting tools and Service Now functionality create script to automate rote tasks being done in ServiceNow.
  • Performs integrations and process automation using ServiceNow Orchestration.
  • Load, manipulate and maintain data between ServiceNow and other systems.
  • Thorough understanding of ServiceNow Security incident response and Vulnerable Item processes
  • Performs system and integration testing with sample and live data Monitor health, usage, and overall compliance of the application.
  • Hands-on design, development, and deployment experience with the ServiceNow platform.


Privacy Notice

Privacy Note: https://ibegin.tcs.com/iBegin/privacy-notice


Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is an equal opportunity employer. We truly believe that employing a diverse workforce is pivotal to our success. Please note, recruiting decisions are always based on applicant’s skills, competencies & experience and potential fitment to the available role

companyDescription:
GBI is a mid-sized European bank established in Amsterdam, the Netherlands since 1990 and serves to retail, corporate and institutional clientele.  We offer financial solutions to our customers and counterparties in the areas of trade and commodity finance, cash management, global market sales, treasury, and structured finance, while maintaining multi-product relationships with local and global financial institutions around the world. GBI also provides targeted retail banking services in the Netherlands and Germany.

We embrace a personable approach towards our clients and counterparties and claim ownership of their challenges as though they are our own. We provide simple yet customized solutions in a complex regulatory and macro-economic space. Our digital solutions and aspirations aim creating and sustaining a unique customer experience.

GBI is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Turkiye Garanti Bankasi A.S. (Garanti) and has presence in Germany, Switzerland and Turkey. Our ultimate parent is Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria (BBVA) as GBI is fully consolidated under Garanti and Garanti is fully consolidated under BBVA. GBI operates in compliance with the Dutch and European Union laws and regulations, and is under the supervision of European Central Bank (ECB), De Nederlandsche Bank (DNB) and De Autoriteit Financiële Markten (AFM).

Join us and release your potential.
companySlogan: Personal. Fair. Responsible.
descriptionHtml:
At Garanti BBVA International (GBI), we are enhancing our resilience and business continuity capabilities to address current and future challenges — including compliance with EU regulations such as the Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA). We are seeking a motivated Intern to join our teams and contribute to the bank’s Business Continuity and Resilience Strategy. The internship is based at our Amsterdam Head Office

What You Will Do

As part of our team, you will:

  • Support the development of a Business Impact Analysis (BIA) tool.
  • Facilitate and perform BIA sessions for critical end-to-end processes with stakeholders (Business Teams, Process Owners, IT, etc.).
  • Extract, visualize, and present BIA results in Power BI dashboards.
  • Assess outcomes to support Business Continuity Plans (BCP) and contribute to periodic testing.
  • Assist the teams in implementing preventive controls, recovery strategies, and contingency plans in line with best practices.
  • Collaborate across departments on resilience-related initiatives.
  • Contribute to Incident Response and Crisis Management activities, including:
  • Updating and refining the Incident Response Plan.
  • Assisting in the design of incident detection and monitoring practices.
  • Helping to establish major incident escalation and triage procedures.
  • Participating in crisis management exercises to improve organizational readiness.


Why this matters

According to industry insights, business continuity is not only about recovery but about maintaining critical operations in the face of disruption — whether from cyberattacks, natural disasters, or operational breakdowns. Effective BCP involves:

Business Impact Analysis (BIA): defining acceptable downtime, recovery time objectives (RTO), recovery point objectives (RPO) and dependencies to systems, third parties etc.

Business Continuity Plans (BCP): ensuring essential services continue during emergencies.

Disaster Recovery Planning: restoring IT systems with clear procedures, communication protocols, and alternate sites.

Incident Response & Crisis Management: ensuring rapid detection, escalation, triage, and coordinated response to incidents that could disrupt operations.

Collaboration & Continuous Improvement: involving stakeholders at all levels to ensure shared ownership and resilience.

What We Are Looking For

  • Enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s program in Business, IT, Risk Management, Security, or a related field.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to think critically and propose practical solutions.
  • Skilled at gathering, interpreting, and presenting data clearly to diverse stakeholders.
  • Familiarity with Excel, Power BI or other data visualization tools is an advantage.
  • Strong motivation to explore risk management, cybersecurity, and resilience frameworks, with a keen interest in EU regulatory standards such as DORA.
  • Proactive and detail-oriented, with a strong willingness to learn and adapt.
  • A self-starter, well-organized, and able to manage multiple tasks and priorities effectively.
  • Excellent English communication skills (written and oral) — essential for engaging with international teams.


What We Offer

  • A challenging internship in an international banking environment.
  • Direct involvement in strategic resilience and regulatory compliance initiatives.
  • Mentorship from experienced professionals.
  • An opportunity to shape resilience practices aligned with global standards and regulations, such as DORA.
  • Full/part time internship for a period of 6-9 months
  • We offer you an environment where you can accelerate your learning curve at a reputable mid-size bank in The Netherlands, part of a global Bank BBVA.
  • Becoming part of a dynamic team in an international working environment.
  • Learning library of Garanti BBVA International
  • Monthly meetings with fellow interns and company leaders
  • Internship gratification of €650, - (Bachelor) €800, - (Master)
  • Compensation Travel Costs
  • Internet costs €45, - per month


Our organization:

Work at Garanti BBVA International

Garanti BBVA International (“GBI”) is a mid-sized European bank established in Amsterdam, the Netherlands since 1990 and serves to corporate and institutional and retail clientele. GBI is a wholly owned subsidiary of Turkiye Garanti Bankasi A.S. (Garanti) and has presence in Germany, Switzerland and Turkey. Our ultimate parent is BBVA. Aa dynamic “global boutique” bank, Garanti BBVA International offers fast, accurate, innovative, tailor-made and country-specific financial solutions in selected business lines though highly skilled and diverse talent management. We embrace creativity, are centered towards teamwork and oriented to create multicultural environment as we believe this to be a crucial element. Our vision is future-oriented and as a one big team GBI strives for corporate excellence and goal achievement. By joining our organization, you will find yourself constantly challenged and must be prone to apply out-of-the box thinking.

Life at Garanti BBVA International

GBI is a place for people who dream big and make their dream their goal. We look for people who will challenge the status quo in order to grow. We stand as one team, one family and together we are empowered to create our unique path, be agile, be curious and innovative. Strong partnerships ensure our success. Strong teams hold our foundation. We believe that diversity drives innovation and fosters personal development. We aim to create a working environment where employees are able to learn through support and coaching while contributing visibly to the success of bank.

Our Core Values

GBI is part of BBVA, an integrated and innovative financial institution that looks to the future, created with the mission of financing people’s dreams. That spirit continues to inspire us today and encourages us to transform people’s lives by helping them make the best decisions to fulfil their dreams. This mission is underpinned by our Purpose: “To bring the age of opportunity to everyone”. We live these values, which make us strong and allow us to achieve our purpose so we can keep creating opportunities.

Customer Comes First

We see our work from the customer's perspective, as we incorporate their point of view in every decision we make and always put their interests first. We meet our customer's needs in an agile way, overcoming any difficulties that arise.

Think Big

We want to amaze our customers with the best solutions to generate unique experiences. To achieve this, we set ambitious and inspirational challenges for ourselves, while questioning everything we do and posing new ways of doing things.

One Team

We are committed both to our individual role and the company's objectives as our own. We work as one team, trust our colleagues and break silos between units and hierarchical barriers.

Application:

If you are ready to join our team and contribute to the success of company, we encourage you to apply by submitting your resume and a cover letter. You have two options to apply:

  • Online Application: Click on the link below to access our online application portal and submit your application.
  • Email Application: Alternatively, you can send your application documents (resume and cover letter) directly to hr@garantibank.eu.


We appreciate your interest in our organization and look forward to reviewing your application. Should you have any questions or require further information, please don't hesitate to reach out.

Please note that we do not appreciate any acquisition efforts for this vacancy by recruitment agencies.

GBI is a wholly owned subsidiary of Turkiye Garanti Bankasi A.S. (Garanti) and has presence in Germany and Turkey. Our ultimate parent is BBVA.

Garanti BBVA International (GBI) creates tailor-made financial solutions for all its clients, across Europe and the globe in the areas of trade and commodity finance, corporate banking and global markets. GBI also provides targeted retail banking services in the Netherlands and Germany.

companyDescription:
Sopra Steria, a major Tech player in Europe with 51,000 employees in nearly 30 countries, is recognised for its consulting, digital services and solutions. It helps its clients drive their digital transformation and obtain tangible and sustainable benefits. The Group provides end-to-end solutions to make large companies and organisations more competitive by combining in-depth knowledge of a wide range of business sectors and innovative technologies with a collaborative approach. Sopra Steria places people at the heart of everything it does and is committed to putting digital to work for its clients in order to build a positive future for all. In 2024, the Group generated revenues of €5.8 billion.

The world is how we shape it
companySlogan: The world is how we shape it.
descriptionHtml:
Bedrijfsomschrijving

Sopra Steria biedt op maat gemaakte end to-end corporate technology en software solutions aan die organisaties helpen gedurfde keuzes te maken en resultaten te boeken. En met succes! Met meer dan 51.000 collega’s in bijna 30 landen behoren we tot de top van de Europese digital solutions leveranciers. Succesvolle organisaties in Europa vertrouwen op onze technologie omdat we blijven streven naar innovatie, samenwerking en zakelijke ontwikkeling. The world is how we shape it. Let’s shape it together

Vacatureomschrijving

Ben jij een hands-on cybersecuritymanager die energie krijgt van eigenaarschap nemen en impact maken? Heb jij de ervaring om complexe projecten te leiden, junior collega’s te begeleiden en bij te dragen aan business development? Dan zijn wij op zoek naar jou!

Wat ga je doen?

  • Leidinggeven aan complexe cybersecurityprojecten bij klanten, van begin tot eind.
  • Actief bijdragen aan business development en pre-sales activiteiten.
  • Junior consultants begeleiden in hun professionele ontwikkeling.
  • Meeschrijven aan offertes en aanbestedingen, afgestemd op klantbehoeften.
  • Innovatie stimuleren en best practices uitdragen binnen de organisatie.
  • Fungeren als trusted advisor voor klanten, waarbij je technische diepgang koppelt aan zakelijke inzichten.

Functie-eisen

Wat breng jij mee?

  • Minimaal 5 jaar ervaring als cybersecurityconsultant of in een vergelijkbare rol.
  • Een proactieve houding met een sterk verantwoordelijkheidsgevoel.
  • Aantoonbare ervaring met projectleiding en het coachen van teamleden.
  • Sterke communicatieve vaardigheden en commercieel inzicht.
  • Affiniteit met zowel technische inhoud als strategische vraagstukken.

We zijn op zoek naar gepassioneerde collega’s die graag de grenzen willen verleggen op het gebied van digitale transformatie en technologie consulting. Bij Sopra Steria krijg je de kans om te werken aan impactvolle projecten en je vaardigheden te ontwikkelen in een constructieve teamomgeving. Jij wilt tot de besten behoren. Dat is waarom jij altijd naar manieren zoekt om jouw kennis uit te breiden en op de hoogte te blijven van de laatste trends en ontwikkelingen. Hou je van een uitdaging en herken je jezelf in (de meeste van) deze omschrijvingen? Dan kijken we nu al uit naar jouw sollicitatie.

Dienstverband: hybride

Locatie: Nederland

Contracturen: 32 – 40 uur per week

Contracttype: vast dienstverband

Opleiding: Hbo of gelijkwaardig

Ervaring: senior

Talen: Nederlands vereist, Engels gewenst

  • Kennis en ervaring met het volgende:
  • Een afgeronde hbo- of wo-opleiding, bij voorkeur met focus op IT of cybersecurity.
  • Minimaal 5 jaar ervaring als cybersecurityspecialist op het gebied van security engineering, monitoring en/of incident response.
  • Certificeringen zoals CISM, CRISC, CISSP of ISO Lead Implementer.

Zie je het al voor je? Veel verschillende projecten, nationaal en internationaal. Een team dat je inspireert en jij in het middelpunt van dit alles. Zo is het om bij Sopra Steria te werken. Bovendien maak je deel uit van een team dat er alles aan doet om impact te maken bij onze klanten.

Aanvullende informatie

Dit krijg je van ons

Bij Sopra Steria zorgen we ervoor dat jij je werk met plezier én in balans kunt doen. Daarom bieden we een aantrekkelijk en flexibel pakket aan arbeidsvoorwaarden:

  • Een vast contract;
  • Een royaal mobiliteitsbudget in de vorm van een leaseregeling met de keuze uit een elektrische leaseauto met NS Business Card of leasefiets of een alternatief vervoersregeling – net wat het beste bij jouw manier van reizen past;
  • Winstdeling van 8% on target (of OTB vast bedrag, afhankelijk van de functie);
  • 27 betaalde vakantiedagen;
  • Flex benefit budget van € 1.800, - per jaar met daar bovenop een extra percentage van jouw salaris. Dit budget kan worden gebruikt voor zaken als aankoop van extra vrije dagen of extra sparen in jouw pensioenregeling;
  • Thuiskantoor met laptop, telefoon en een maandelijkse internetvergoeding;
  • Hybride werken: thuis of op kantoor, afhankelijk van wat jij nodig hebt;
  • Ontwikkelmogelijkheden via trainingen, kennisdelingssessies en inspirerende (netwerk)events;
  • Sociale activiteiten met collega’s – van gezellige borrels tot sportieve of inhoudelijke uitjes;
  • Een flexibele pensioenregeling, waaraan jij op maat en passend bij jou, bijdragen kan toevoegen.
  • Je kunt deelnemen aan de collectieve zorgverzekering.

Lijkt deze job iets voor jou?

Ben je klaar om samen met ons de wereld vorm te geven? Fantastisch! We kijken nu al uit naar jouw sollicitatie.

______

Sopra Steria is een werkgever die gelijke kansen biedt. Alle gekwalificeerde sollicitanten worden in aanmerking genomen voor een job zonder rekening te houden met leeftijd, afkomst, nationaliteit, huidskleur, verlof voor familie- of medische zorg, geslachtsidentiteit of -uitdrukking, genetische informatie, immigratiestatus, burgerlijke staat, medische toestand, nationale herkomst, fysieke of mentale handicap, politieke voorkeur, beschermde veteranen- of militaire status, ras, etniciteit, religie, geslacht (inclusief zwangerschap), seksuele geaardheid of enig ander kenmerk dat wordt beschermd door toepasselijke lokale wetten, voorschriften en verordeningen. We koesteren een werkomgeving die inclusief is en alle verschillen respecteert.

descriptionHtml:
Senior Detection Engineer


Do you want to fully understand a job and organization, or would you rather be intrigued and explore that feeling further in a direct conversation? We believe in the latter and introduce refreshingly short job descriptions.


For our partner, Fox-IT, we are looking for a Senior Detection Engineer who enjoys working on advanced detection projects. In this role, you’ll be part of the Global Detection Engineering Team, focusing on detection capabilities for global clients.


You need:


  • To work with advanced technology.
  • An analytical and out-of-the-box mindset.
  • A position at one of the most reputable cybersecurity companies in the Netherlands.


What you get based on your experience and skills:


  • Salary range of €4.200 - €5.400.
  • Hybrid working setup from Delft.
  • Performance-based bonus.


About the organization:


  • Fox-IT provides innovative detection solutions as part of 24/7 monitoring for clients worldwide.
  • A leading player in cybersecurity, helping organizations defend against modern cyber threats.
  • Known for its advanced approach and commitment to protecting global digital assets.


Interested? We receive many applications, so don’t wait too long!

companyDescription:
Amoria Bond is an award-winning international specialist recruitment company with the ambitious goal of Progressing Lives Everywhere. 

From our offices in Manchester, Amsterdam, Cologne, Singapore and US, we deliver both permanent and temporary contract staffing solutions in Advanced Engineering, Technology and Energy. We’re proud to work with specialists in pioneering sectors that create a cleaner, better world. 

Our company purpose of Progressing Lives Everywhere, with our PROFES values (Positivity, Respect, Ownership, Fun, Excellence, Success), influences everything we do. We hold ourselves fully accountable to this through our Best Service Charter, which sets out the quality, standards and expectations we promise our clients, candidates, colleagues, and wider community. 

Our strong track record of international expansion accompanies our commitments to give back as we grow, through the Amoria Bond Charitable Trust and our tree planting pledge: we’ve planted over 163,000 trees worldwide since July 2020. 

As founding partner signatories of the Diversity & Inclusion Charter, we provide a high-performing, supportive & inclusive working environment built around our strong value-led culture: 97% of employees enjoy working at Amoria Bond, and 98% agree we provide an inclusive workplace for all employees, regardless of background or differences. 

Our culture of progression and development means we invest significantly in our people with award-winning training and a clear progression plan for every employee. In fact, 90% of our leaders have successfully progressed from trainees, including members of our Executive Board. Thanks to initiatives like this, we’ve won multiple awards, including APSCo DE's "Staffing Company of the Year" in 2024, and have been featured for the seventh consecutive year in the Recruiter Hot 100 in 2025.

descriptionHtml:
SOC Analyst – 12+ Months – Amsterdam

SIEM – Threat hunting - Incident Response

Are you a SOC Analyst ready to be on the frontline of cyber defense? Our client is looking for a SOC Analyst to join their Security Operations Center to help detect, investigate and respond to threats in real time. Our client operates in the financial industry and has a large security team which has doubled in the last two years


Required Experience:

  • SOC Analyst experience
  • Experience with Elastic SIEM
  • Knowledge of threat detection, classification, escalation, and response
  • Hands-on experience with threat hunting activities
  • Skilled in creating and optimizing detection rules
  • Comfortable reporting findings to internal stakeholders

  • Key Words: SOC – Elastic SIEM – Threat Hunting – Incident – Security Operations – Cybersecurity

    If you're the SOC Analyst we're looking for? Send your CV and contact details to fidan.jafarli@amoriabond.com and I will contact you as soon as I can!

This is sometimes the case for unsuccessful applications to one of our vacancies. We apologise for this and can reassure you that we will keep your details and contact you in the future should any suitable positions arise.

    Amoria Bond operates as an employment agency and employment business. No terminology in this advert is intended to discriminate on the grounds of age or experience, and we confirm that we are happy to accept applications from persons of any age or experience for this role.

Aan de hand van een dynamische mix van workshops, cases en zelfstudie stomen wij je in korte tijd klaar om goed voorbereid van start te gaan bij jouw opdrachtgever. Jij wordt voor jouw start gekoppeld aan een project bij een van onze partners, daardoor kunnen wij vanaf dag één direct gefocust van start met jouw opleiding. 

De traineeships zijn altijd specialistisch én gericht op de specifieke behoefte van onze partners. Je wordt opgeleid in een functie zoals: security engineer, data engineer, IT-consultant of functioneel beheerder.  

Wil jij kennismaken en ontdekken wat wij voor jou kunnen doen? Neem dan contact op met ons!
descriptionHtml:
Ga aan de slag als Security Analist!


De cyberweerbaarheid vergroten. Dat is de missie van het Security Operations Center (SOC). Daar ga jij als security analist op locatie mee aan de slag. In deze rol zet jij jouw analytische én communicatieve vaardigheden in voor de cyberveiligheid. Dit doe je met behulp van de meest geavanceerde tools en besteden we voortdurend aandacht aan jouw professionele en persoonlijke ontwikkeling, zodat jij steeds beter in je vak wordt.

Als security analist heb je een breed takenpakket. In deze functie ben je verantwoordelijk voor het monitoren van cyberdreigingen en het afhandelen van securitymeldingen van het team en onze partners. Op basis van triage beslis je hoe deze meldingen moeten worden opgevolgd. Ook in de opvolging blijf je als kennispartner betrokken bij een cyberincident. Jij houdt je in het kort bezig met het:


  • identificeren van dreigingen, zoals kwetsbaarheden en malware, die vermeld staan in rapportages van externe partijen en in interne rapportages van beheerteams en securitymanagement;
  • opvolgen van securitymeldingen en incidenten met behulp van veiliggesteld materiaal zoals logfiles of malware;
  • onderzoeken van de oorzaken van securitymeldingen en incidenten om de impactanalyse te kunnen bepalen;
  • verbeteren van de detectie op dreiging en daarmee de doorontwikkeling van het SOC.


Dit vragen wij


  • Minimaal HBO-werk en denkniveau en bij voorkeur een HBO-diploma in de richting van ICT of security.
  • Minimaal 1 jaar werkervaring in een vergelijkbare functie of in verschillende operationele functies binnen het domein van ICT-beveiliging.
  • Ervaring met Security Monitoring, SIEM-Tooling (zoals Splunk) en Linux- en Windowssystemen.
  • Ervaring met het herkennen van aanvalstechnieken (technisch en in de vorm van social engineering) en het overbrengen hiervan aan collega’s.
  • Bij voorkeur 1 of meerdere securitycertificeringen, zoals GIAC GSEC, GCIH, GMON, GCIA.


Hier kom je te werken


Je gaat aan de slag bij het Security Operations Center (SOC), onderdeel van de Chief Technology Office (CTO). Met zo’n zestien collega’s vormen we een divers team qua leeftijd en culturele achtergrond. Er heerst een informele werksfeer waarin mensen elkaar vertrouwen en op elkaar kunnen bouwen. In ons team streven we ernaar om elkaar te versterken en uit te dagen, zodat we continu groeien en scherp blijven. Bij ons hechten we veel waarde aan een goede balans tussen werk en privé, waarbij we respect hebben voor ieders persoonlijke situatie. We hebben een hechte band en sluiten de week het liefst gezamenlijk af met een drankje.


Benieuwd naar meer informatie? Mail dan naar jsaffrie@vibegroup.com of bel 06-41554012.

descriptionHtml:
Job description | Senior IT Administrator

 

ABOUT ELTEMATE

We are ELTEMATE – A Hogan Lovells Technology Brand. Our goal is to make clients’ lives easier by delivering practical solutions to everyday problems. We combine a deep understanding of our clients' legal needs with the speed and innovation of a technology startup. Our portfolio covers a large spectrum of legal tech solutions including artificial intelligence, eDiscovery, information analysis, regulatory updates, databases, deal rooms, case management, document automation, risk assessment, reporting, and apps.

 

 

ROLE DESCRIPTION

We are looking for a Senior IT Administrator to join our dynamic team. You will be the main contact person for anything relating to hardware and software assets, IT Security responsibilities and IT-related documentation.



DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Build and maintain the internal IT structure of the enterprise.
  • Work with networks spread across all locations globally.
  • Troubleshoot and repair day-to-day issues.
  • Establish and manage onboarding and off-boarding processes.
  • Utilise our MDM platform (Jamf Pro) to configure all computers and mobiles across our offices together with managing policies and their groups.
  • Maintain a high standard of customer service, satisfaction, and support to all end users across all offices and beyond.
  • Utilize scripting languages to automate routine tasks, such as system provisioning, patch management, and log analysis
  • Contribute to implementing security policies, data handling guidelines, and coordinated incident response efforts.
  • Ensure that the organization's IT systems and processes are aligned with internal security policies and guidelines.



REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE

  • 5+ years of experience in the IT Support/Administration environment.
  • Knowledge of macOS and Jamf Pro.
  • Knowledge of networking technologies.

descriptionHtml:
Introduction

In the role of SOC Engineer, you are responsible for monitoring the security of Stater’s IT environment and following up on detected incidents. You will also proactively work on resolving security issues and improving IT security.


Who are we?

Who is Stater? You may not have heard of us before, so we are happy to explain. At Stater, we develop and deliver mortgage services and platforms for external lenders. With our products and services, we manage 40% of mortgages in the Netherlands. Working at Stater means working at an organization that is deeply involved in society, with products that indirectly or directly affect many Dutch households. Our clients include major Dutch banks and other financial institutions.


What will you do?

You will work in Stater’s Security Operations Center (SOC). The SOC consists of six people who work in an agile environment alongside the Cyber Advisory Team of five colleagues, forming together the IT Security team within IT Fundamentals (ITF). The IT Security team reports to a Product Owner (PO) and a People Lead (PL).


You will collaborate with other ITF teams, such as Core Infrastructure, Core Cloud, or Team IAM, to resolve and prevent incidents. ITF is responsible for building, managing, and supporting all IT components at Stater.


As a SOC Engineer, you are the first point of contact within the Security Operations Center. You play a crucial role in monitoring, analyzing, and responding to security incidents within Stater’s IT environment. You are responsible for responding to suspicious activities and initiating the appropriate follow-up to ensure the cybersecurity of our systems. You will also work with our security partner to further mature our SOC by creating and maintaining use-cases and playbooks.


Tasks and responsibilities:

  • Monitor networks, systems, and applications for suspicious activities using SIEM solutions.
  • Analyze security alerts and assess threat levels.
  • Escalate incidents to the appropriate teams.
  • Maintain incident reports, use-cases, and playbooks.
  • Support the execution of response procedures during cyber attacks.
  • Be a member of the Computer Incident Response Team (CIRT).
  • Collaborate with other teams within IT and IT Security to minimize risks.
  • Proactively contribute ideas to improve detection and response processes.


Who are we looking for?

You can work in a structured way in a sometimes hectic environment. You are flexible enough to adjust your priorities when circumstances demand it.


Requirements:


  • HBO-level education, preferably in ICT or Cybersecurity.
  • At least 3 years of work experience, financial sector experience is a plus.
  • Knowledge of networks, operating systems (Windows/Linux), and IT security concepts.
  • Experience with monitoring tools and SIEM platforms is required.
  • Knowledge and experience with Azure, MS Sentinel, and Defender XDR.\
  • SC-200 and/or AZ-500 certifications are a plus.
  • Programming using KQL is a plus.
  • Knowledge of Jira, Agile, working in sprints and Program Increments.
  • Knowledge of ISO27001, SOC2, GDPR, and DORA.
  • Strong analytical skills and stress resistance.


What do we offer?

  • A challenging role and a working environment where you feel at home, of course, but the other employment conditions are also important. Here are some of our benefits:
  • An organization that puts you first with maximum attention to your development. Our Stater Learning Center provides you with the space to grow personally and professionally at our expense. Internal career growth is encouraged and facilitated.
  • Our salary is composed of two components. In addition to a gross monthly salary between €4,586 and €5,733 (scale 21), we offer a flexible deployment budget (PDI) worth 18% of your gross salary. How would you like to use this budget? Extra vacation days, payment in cash, or another option?
  • NS Business card or a travel allowance.
  • Hybrid working is completely normal at Stater. We provide a good home office allowance and a home workspace.
  • Attractive collective discounts on health insurance and home insurance – extra security with a nice discount!
  • A good pension scheme – we find it important that you benefit from your efforts later in life.


Interested?

Can we welcome you as a new colleague, and does this role fit your growth ambitions? Please contact our IT recruiter Arsalan Seidony (arsalan.seidony@stater.nl

)

companyDescription:
As a new generation medical device company, Applied Medical is focused on meeting three fundamental healthcare needs: enhanced clinical outcomes, cost containment and unrestricted choice. Our vertically integrated programs in design, development, manufacturing, and distribution enable us to consistently meet these objectives. As a vertically integrated company, we manufacture all of our products in-house at our corporate headquarters in Rancho Santa Margarita, California, and our European headquarters in Amersfoort, Netherlands with distribution centers around the world.

Applied manufactures surgical devices for a wide range of medical specialties. Each year, Applied dedicates, on average, 20% of our revenue to product development and projects that increase the efficiency of our manufacturing operations. As such, we are able to fuel the continuous development of new surgical technologies.

Applied is proud to have a significant and sustainable impact on healthcare. We address patient needs with advanced technologies that enhance surgical outcomes while also meeting our customers’ pressing economic needs.

Applied team members are part of one of the fastest growing and most progressive organizations in the healthcare field. We are proud to have a culture strongly committed to ethical business practices, mutual respect, teamwork, and caring about our customers and our community.

Applied's team members come from a wide range of experience, and their perspectives are crucial to the continued success of our organization. Applied respects each team member’s opinion and fosters a culture of open communication across all levels and departments.

We are looking for exceptional people who share in our mission to deliver breakthrough technologies that enhance surgical and clinical care. We seek creative problem-solvers from a variety of career paths and backgrounds who are interested in making a meaningful, positive difference.

descriptionHtml:
Are you eager to be a driving force in our in-house IT security and compliance activities? Together with your colleagues within IT you will be responsible to implement and maintain security measures to protect company computer networks and systems and the information contained within them. To excel in this position, you are proactive, a real team player and open to be flexible when needed. You like challenges and can always come up with the best solution. If you come across a security risk, you are the best person to coordinate with various teams, resolving the issue at hand in a timely manner.

Key Responsibilities

  • Ensuring security risks are identified and define technical improvement/action plans
  • Automate the identification of security events by defining rules in Security systems
  • Perform regular root causes analysis and suggest actions to be taken based upon outcome
  • Promote and assess compliance with current rules, policies, and standards
  • Collect, compile, summarize and record security data and trends
  • Foresee potential issues and escalate concerns

Wat heb je nodig

  • Bachelors/HBO level education, preferably in Cybersecurity
  • 1+ years of working experience in IT Security
  • Experience with working with various (network) security solutions such as SIEM, IDS, NDR and EDR systems
  • Security certification in CEH, CISSP or CASP is preferred
  • Fluency in English, both written and spoken

Wat wij bieden

  • An international working environment, fueled by respect and a healthy work-life balance tailored to the business and team member’s needs
  • 27 paid vacation days (based on fulltime contract) with option to purchase five additional days
  • Commuting allowance of €0,23/km or an NS Business card subscription
  • A wide variety of internal trainings and development possibilities and a tuition program for external trainings
  • The opportunity to pro-actively work on your vitality and fitness
  • State-of-the-art facilities, including green areas, in-house restaurant and gym
  • Additional benefits, such as:
    • Attractive pension scheme (your contribution at 4%, ours at 12%)
    • €50 net monthly health insurance contribution
    • Two (paid) days per year dedicated to volunteering activities
    • Several fun company events and meaningful initiatives organized throughout the year
At Applied Medical, we are committed to make a meaningful, positive difference, each and every day. Within our European headquarters in Amersfoort (NL), we develop, manufacture and support the implementation of our advanced medical devices across Europe and the MENA region. Applied’s team members enjoy working in an environmentally friendly and international environment, in which people of diverse backgrounds are valued, challenged, and acknowledged. We support our team members by giving them opportunities of ongoing learning and education, possibilities to take part in community volunteering activities and a variety of onsite exercise classes and health activities.

If you are passionate about working in an organization that allows you to make decisions based upon what is right for the patient, customer, community and environment, Applied Medical is the place for you.

Interested?

Would you like to be part of our expanding team? If the answer is yes, we'd love to get to know you and look forward to hearing from you!

For more information regarding our hiring process, click here. If you have any questions, feel free to contact us via TA-Europe@appliedmedical.com or +31 (0)33 7548713.

companyDescription:
Founded in 1993, Prodrive Technologies designs and manufactures high-tech electronics, software, and mechatronic products and systems. We operate five dedicated R&D programs and three highly automated manufacturing sites as well as sales offices around the world. 

Everything centers on the customer, our passion for technology, and on constantly improving our business processes. This ‘DNA’ has been crucial to our company for three decades and it is the foundation of our success as well as that of our customers. Producing everything we need in-house helps us to establish smart ways of optimizing, integrating, and robotizing, and gives our products exceptional price-performance ratio.

Our mission is to contribute to innovations that tackle major challenges in society. In other words, we create meaningful technologies that make the world work.

descriptionHtml:
Do you have a passion for maintaining high safety standards and the determination to analyze and resolve puzzles and issues? The cybersecurity landscape changes rapidly, and we are looking for someone who is passionate about continuous learning and adapting to new threats and technologies. Do you think you are the perfect fit? Then we invite you to join our Security Operations Center (SOC) team.


We are Prodrive Technologies

We are dedicated to creating meaningful technologies that make the world work. We want to contribute to innovations that tackle major challenges in society, such as improving the quality of medical imaging. Besides, our technologies contribute to reducing the global dependency on fossil fuels and minimizing human exposure to air pollution. In other words, we create meaningful technologies that make the world work.


About the role

As part of our growing SOC team, we are looking for a proactive and motivated SOC Analyst to help safeguard our infrastructure and assets from emerging cyber threats. In this role, you will work closely with IT, development teams, and other departments to enhance security measures, improve incident management, and ensure compliance with industry standards.


Key Responsibilities

  • Continually enhance the SOC’s detection capabilities by fine-tuning existing SIEM rules and developing new ones to ensure accurate and timely identification of security threats
  • Develop and implement automated playbooks and workflows to streamline incident response, reduce response time, and free up resources for proactive security initiatives
  • Identify, track, and help remediate vulnerabilities that pose risks to our systems, ensuring that critical patches and fixes are applied in a timely manner to reduce exposure
  • Stay up-to-date with the latest trends in the cybersecurity landscape. Participate in training, certifications, and conferences to continually enhance your skills and knowledge
  • Analyze detected events, correlate information, and translate findings into preventive actions to fortify our security posture


Profile

  • Strong understanding of SOC operations, security incident management, and incident response workflows
  • Knowledge of SIEM tools and threat detection strategies
  • Familiarity with vulnerability management and patching processes
  • Ability to develop automated playbooks and workflows to improve SOC efficiency
  • Solid understanding of cybersecurity trends, technologies, and best practices
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills, able to work effectively with cross-functional teams
  • Eagerness to continue growing and enhancing your cybersecurity knowledge through ongoing learning and certifications
  • This is not a remote job so you’re willing to work at the office
  • Able to speak and write in the English language

Our Specialties:

descriptionHtml:
Incident Responder – Amsterdam (Hybrid)


My client, a leading organisation in the financial services space, is seeking an experienced Incident Responder to join their security team in Amsterdam. This individual will act as the main escalation point between 1st line analysts and an outsourced SOC provider, playing a pivotal role in ensuring incidents are investigated, contained, and resolved effectively.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Serve as the escalation point for incidents raised by 1st line analysts and the SOC.
  • Lead investigations into complex security events, providing detailed analysis and clear remediation steps.
  • Collaborate with both internal teams and the SOC partner to manage containment and recovery.
  • Perform log analysis using SIEM platforms (Splunk, QRadar, Sentinel, or similar).
  • Lead triage and response activities, including malware analysis, phishing investigations, and insider threat detection.
  • Coordinate with SOC and internal stakeholders to contain and remediate threats.
  • Contribute to and improve IR playbooks, threat hunting procedures, and incident post-mortems.
  • Deliver timely, concise communication to stakeholders during and after incidents.


Candidate Profile:

  • Strong background in incident response having already had experince being a senior escalation point.
  • Previous experience within financial services or cryptocurrency environments is highly advantageous.
  • Familiarity with SOC operations and escalation procedures.
  • Confident communicator, able to bridge the gap between technical teams and business stakeholders.
  • Must be based in the Netherlands and able to work 2 days per week onsite in Amsterdam.


Additional Information:

  • Visa sponsorship is not available for this position.

descriptionHtml:
Als jij gepassioneerd bent over het oplossen van complexe Cyber Security incidenten en het begeleiden van organisaties bij hun ‘incident readiness’ strategie, dan is dit de perfecte rol voor jou!

Jouw uitdagingen

Ben jij een daadkrachtig, gedreven leider die ons Incident Response Team nodig heeft? Als jij gepassioneerd bent over het oplossen van complexe Cyber Security incidenten en het begeleiden van organisaties bij hun ‘incident readiness’ strategie, dan is dit de perfecte rol voor jou!

Jouw Uitdagingen

  • Begeleid (sr.) Consultants bij het onderzoeken van Cyber Security incidenten en coördineer herstelwerkzaamheden.
  • Werk (internationaal) samen in een gespecialiseerd team van Incident Responders onder tijdsdruk in een crisissituatie.
  • Breng innovatieve Incident Response oplossingen naar klanten op basis van de laatste technologie in samenwerking met een team enthousiaste consultants.
  • Communiceer met zowel interne als externe stakeholders over incidenten, onze adviesdiensten en daaraan gerelateerde strategische vraagstukken.


Dit neem jij mee

  • Een afgeronde Master of Bachelor-opleiding in een relevant gebied (bijvoorbeeld Computer Science, Cyber Security, Elektrotechniek, etc.).
  • Je hebt reeds 6-9 jaar relevante ervaring in crisis management, IR consultancy of vergelijkbare rol.
  • Affiniteit en ervaring met techniek en de motivatie om ons dienstenportfolio uit te breiden en naar onze klanten te brengen.
  • Uitstekende communicatievaardigheden in zowel Nederlands als Engels in woord en geschrift.
  • Je bent een goede coach en crisis manager, kan goed functioneren in een dynamische omgeving en hebt naast een sterk analytisch vermogen ook een hoog verantwoordelijkheidsgevoel.
  • Eerdere ervaring met forensische tools en technische Cyber Security oplossingen is een pré.
  • Woonachtig in Nederland en bereidheid te reizen en op diverse locaties te werken, soms ook buiten kantoortijden.


Wat ga je doen?

In de rol van Manager Incident Response bij KPMG, speel je een essentiële rol tijdens crisissituaties bij toonaangevende organisaties. Je geeft leiding aan proactieve en reactieve Incident Response opdrachten. Daarbij ben jij de centrale spil tussen de klant en je team.

  • Je onderzoekt met je team digitaal forensische sporen en analyseert technische beveiligingsmaatregelen naar aanleiding van incidenten.
  • Je evalueert de veiligheid van kritieke systemen, identificeert kwetsbaarheden in bedrijfsprocessen en ondersteunt bij verschillende herstelactiviteiten. Daarbij maak je gebruik van een breed scala aan technische tooling en gedreven (sr.) consultants en managers.
  • Je bent adviseert de klant ten aanzien van het cyber incident en coördineert de verschillende werkstromen en stakeholders.
  • Je meet de organisatie haar weerbaarheid bij cyber incidenten (incident readiness) en adviseert de klant over de praktisch en strategische vervolgstappen om de weerbaarheid te verhogen.


Hierbij zal je vaak schakelen tussen verschillende communicatiestijlen. Het ene moment spreek je met niet-technische besluitvormers over prioriteiten en mogelijke oplossingen, het andere moment ga je dieper in op specifieke security-instellingen met IT- of beveiligingspersoneel.

Als onderdeel van ons brede KPMG Cyber & TechLaw-team werk je samen met enthousiaste, ambitieuze professionals. Onze expertise varieert van strategische vraagstukken, implementatie van technische security platformen tot juridisch advies rond wet- en regelgeving. De diversiteit van ons team zorgt voor een dynamische en inspirerende omgeving waarin je kunt groeien en ontwikkelen.

Dit bieden wij

  • Een salarisrange van EUR 5.500 - EUR 6.500 bruto per maand, winstuitkering, een vaste onkostenvergoeding en een thuiswerkvergoeding.
  • 30 vakantiedagen (op fulltime basis) en de mogelijkheid om meer dagen te kopen of je vakantiedagen te verkopen.
  • Een leaseauto+NS business kaart of de mobiliteitsvergoeding
  • Een flexibel pensioen zonder verplichte eigen bijdrage.
  • Een uitgebreid aanbod van trainingen en opleidingen die bijdragen aan jouw professionele en persoonlijke ontwikkeling.
  • Aandacht voor vitaliteit! Sporten op het kantoor in Amstelveen of korting op een sportschool bij jou in de buurt én toegang tot coaching, gezondheids- en vitaliteitprogramma’s
  • Together’ is één van onze kernwaarden. Je kunt dus rekenen op verschillende sociale activiteiten zoals teamuitjes, borrels en evenementen samen met je collega’s

companyDescription:
At LocalStack, we provide the #1 platform for local cloud development & testing, offering the most advanced cloud emulation for AWS services. Our aim is to assist individual cloud developers and enterprises in becoming solution nimble, optimizing costs, reducing contention, and delivering resilient and secure value to their customers.

LocalStack is ideal for designing and testing highly resilient and secure solutions on your local machine before live deployment. Our platform facilitates integration testing of cloud solutions both locally and in CI/CD environments, enhancing developer velocity and feature quality.

With LocalStack, you fully emphasize on black-box solution tests that fully exercise the inputs and outputs of your system, rather than solely focusing on unit tests.

Our solution also enables rapid design and development of sophisticated cloud solutions with little to no changes needed for IaC pipelines. In addition, by emulating cloud services locally, LocalStack enables additional features and workflows that are not feasible on the cloud, such as local chaos engineering, IAM security testing, team collaboration, and more!
companySlogan: Run your cloud & serverless apps locally to reduce development time and increase product velocity with LocalStack!
descriptionHtml:
We are a fast-growing Series A startup building cutting-edge technology to revolutionize cloud development processes and support highly efficient dev&test feedback loops. We've closed our last $25mil round in Q4 2024, led by Notable Capital, CRV and Heavybit.

At its core, LocalStack provides a high-fidelity emulator and local cloud development platform. Imagine developing cloud applications and data pipelines entirely on your local machine within a lightweight cloud sandbox, running in Docker!

Our mission is to empower developers to rapidly build and test their cloud applications, allowing for a more enjoyable dev experience, and saving valuable time and resources.

LocalStack has a large and active open-source community (57k+ stars on GitHub) with over 100k active users worldwide and 290M+ downloads to date. Our customer base ranges from SMBs to Global Fortune 500 companies.

We are sustainably growing our globally distributed team across sectors

LocalStack is headquartered in Zurich/Switzerland 🇨🇭, with a main engineering office in Vienna/Austria 🇦🇹 and remote team members from 🇺🇸the US, 🇫🇷FR, 🇬🇧UK, 🇨🇦CA, 🇪🇸ES, and many more countries.

👉Check our Notion Candidate Handbook and our GitHub!

This is the right opportunity for a person with 7+ years in a security engineering or security compliance role, experience leading vendor risk assessments and building compliance frameworks from the ground up, a strong background in API design and build, as well as a strong background in DevSecOps, incident response, and pragmatic, risk-driven security leadership.

Requirements

✅ What you will be working on/responsible for

  • Ensure robust security posture of our product, across the various components (in particular, the LocalStack emulators, the LocalStack Cloud platform, as well as our data warehouse)
  • Lead initiatives for incident monitoring, intrusion detection, and vulnerability management
  • Define and implement regular security auditing procedures across systems and access controls
  • Deliver a sustainable, scalable process for vendor risk assessments and other security-related initiatives (e.g., via tooling, delegation, or automation) including completing and submitting vendor risk assessments to support our sales process
  • Ensure secure configurations and permission models, while collaborating with the engineering teams
  • Identify gaps between claimed and actual compliance and propose/lead corrective actions
  • Own documentation of security controls, configurations, and policies
  • Engage with internal stakeholders to evaluate different security threats and attack vectors
  • Generate and distribute internal audit and compliance reports in regular intervals

✅ Experience we expect you to bring to the role

  • Expert knowledge of threat modelling, vulnerability management, and tools like intrusion detection, network security, or Linux/Unix OS hardening
  • Practical experience with cloud security (AWS preferred)
  • Good knowledge of common standards (e.g., SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR), even if not formally certified
  • Strong documentation skills and ability to make complex topics accessible to non-experts
  • Good understanding of US and EU security and compliance expectations
  • Prior engineering experience strongly preferred (even if no longer coding daily)
  • Proactive, pragmatic, and capable of risk-based decision-making

🪢 Values we hold in LocalStack

care: we create with compassion. We prioritize empathy and understanding in every interaction. By genuinely caring for our team, customers, and community, we create an environment where people thrive and impactful work flourishes

ownership: we own the outcome. We take responsibility for our work and are passionate about its impact. We foster autonomy, inspire ambition, encourage ownership, and empower everyone to unlock their potential and make an impact.

openness: we build trust together. We build trust through open communication and honest feedback. By sharing ideas and embracing diverse perspectives, we create stronger, more connected teams that work toward shared goals.

courage: we dare to innovate. We embrace bold challenges and take calculated risks to move the needle. We step outside our comfort zones, experiment fearlessly, and turn setbacks into springboards for growth.

excellence: we chase the extraordinary. We chase excellence by pushing boundaries and delivering results that go beyond the ordinary, constantly raising the bar and striving for greatness in everything we do. Excellence is not just the outcome, itʼs how we approach every task with purpose, passion, and a commitment to delivering exceptional value.

Benefits

🎁 Benefits

  • Fully remote
  • Competitive salary
  • Annual company retreat
  • 2 extra company-wide holidays
  • Friendly and inclusive workplace culture (community guilds and online company events)

We'd love to hear from you! Join us in shaping the future of cloud development at LocalStack.

To apply, follow the LI application process or apply on our career page. Make sure to include a short motivation outlining why you are the perfect candidate for this role.

If your profile looks like a good match we will be in touch to organize further steps within 2 weeks.

Please note, that due to a high volume of candidates, we cannot offer personalized feedback to each candidate.

descriptionHtml:
Role: Cyber Incident Response Lead

Location: Utrecht, hybrid

Company: Global Security and Intelligence Leader


Lawrence Harvey are partnered with a leader in the Cyber Security and Intelligence space. We are searching for a skilled Cyber Incident Response Lead to come and work on high-impact Incident Response projects whilst facilitating the growth of their IR practice in the BeNeLux region.


The Role:

  • Grow our BeNeLux Incident Response practice
  • Build & manage partnerships (insurance, legal, ecosystem players)
  • Collect, analyze & preserve technical evidence
  • Investigate breaches & assess impact (confidentiality, integrity, availability)
  • Deploy & manage tools (EDR, SIEM, forensic platforms, etc.)
  • Advise clients on containment & recovery
  • Communicate technical findings to executives


Requirements:

  • Incident response experience (technical &/or commercial)
  • Ideally: partnerships experience with insurers/legal sector
  • Strong written & verbal communication skills (Dutch & English)
  • Endpoint, network, malware & cloud forensics
  • Enterprise security tools (SIEM, EDR, IDS/IPS, DLP)
  • Windows/Linux administration, AD, email & network infrastructure
  • Virtualization & cloud technologies
  • Relevant certifications (GCFE, GCFA, OSCP, CISSP, etc.) are advantageous!
  • Alternatively, a HBO/WO degree is a plus


Apply using the link if you're excited about joining a true leader in the space and helping facilitate their growth!

Role: Cyber Incident Response Consultant

Location: Utrecht, hybrid


Due to a strong influx in customer demand, this leader within the Security & Intelligence space are looking to bolster their Incident Response teams. We are looking for skilled Cyber Incident Response Consultants who want to work on high-impact projects whilst working alongside a team of experts.


The Role:

  • Collect and preserve technical evidence from client environments
  • Analyze evidence using forensic and log analysis tools
  • Investigate breaches to assess impact (confidentiality, integrity, availability)
  • Gather threat intelligence to support decision-making under pressure
  • Advise clients on containment actions to limit impact
  • Deploy and manage tools such as EDR, SIEM, and forensic platforms
  • Support recovery of IT infrastructure during/after attacks
  • Translate technical findings into clear takeaways for executives


Requirements:

  • Endpoint, malware, network, cloud, or mobile forensics
  • Evidence handling & preservation
  • ICS/OT environments
  • Enterprise security tools (SIEM, EDR, IDS/IPS, DLP, etc.)
  • Windows/Linux administration, Active Directory, identity management
  • Network infrastructure (routers, gateways, switches)
  • Virtualization (VMWare, ESXi, etc.)
  • Email infrastructure (Office 365, Exchange)
  • Remote access technologies
  • Industry Certifications are advantageous (GCFE, GCFA, EnCE, CFSR, CISSP, GREM, CCNA, MCFE, OSCP, Network+, Security)


Proficiency in spoken/written Dutch & English is essential for this role. You must also be legally authorised to work in the Netherlands.


Interested? Apply using the link!

Not for you? Forward to someone who could be a good match!

Job Opportunity: Incident Responder – FinTech (Amsterdam)


We are seeking an experienced Incident Response Engineer to join a rapidly growing FinTech company based in Amsterdam. This is a unique opportunity to work at the cutting edge of cybersecurity and blockchain technology, supporting one of the most exciting FinTech's.


Key Responsibilities

  • Lead incident response investigations from detection through containment, remediation, and reporting.
  • Perform forensic analysis, vulnerability assessments, and contribute to ongoing penetration testing programs.
  • Collaborate closely with security, engineering, and compliance teams to ensure alignment with international standards.
  • Translate technical findings into clear communication for stakeholders, both technical and non-technical.
  • Drive post-incident reviews and help integrate lessons learned into risk management and security frameworks.


What We’re Looking For

  • Proven experience in cybersecurity incident response, ideally within financial services or fintech,
  • Strong understanding of ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II, or similar compliance environments.
  • Familiarity with cryptocurrency security risks such as wallet management, exchange vulnerabilities, and API threats.
  • Solid technical background in forensics, log analysis, and vulnerability management.
  • Excellent communication skills under pressure; able to work cross-functionally in high-stakes situations.
  • Relevant certifications (e.g., CISSP, GCIH, GCFE) are highly desirable.

descriptionHtml:
Tangent International are looking for a Senior Digital Forensic and Incident Response Consultant to work a 12 month extendable contract based in Amsterdam (Remote with requirement for occasional travel)


Role Description:

You will be operating as a Senior Digital Forensic Incident Response (IR) Investigator within the Threat Research Advisory Center (VTRAC). As a DFIR specialist you’ll be expected to serve as a tactical arm of the team, conducting live incident response, computer forensic analysis, data recovery, and other IT investigative work. Due to the inherent volatility of investigative response work, you will be expected to discharge the various responsibilities assigned to their role while successfully managing a variable case load.

The role may require 24/7 activity and travel to customer sites in the UK or overseas at short notice.


In this role, you will be responsible for integrity in analysis, quality in client deliverables, as well as gathering case-load intelligence. The position will operate in a close team of computer forensics, fraud examiners, and other IT investigative experts, as well as customer management, counsel, human resources, and other IT technical personnel.


As a client-facing expert within the VTRAC team, you will be delivering proactive and reactive Digital Forensics and Incident Response (DFIR) services. This role is responsible for architecting cyber resilience for clients through strategic planning and assessment, pressure-testing defenses via advanced breach simulations, and providing decisive leadership as an Incident Commander during major cyber crises.


VTRAC provides an unparalleled, intelligence-driven approach to helping organizations prepare, respond, and recover from the most sophisticated cyber threats. This is an opportunity to work on the most challenging and impactful cases alongside the best in the business.


Core Deliverables & Responsibilities:

This is a senior, hands-on consulting role focused on delivering specific, high-value outcomes for enterprise clients.


In this role your responsibilities are:

● Reactive Forensic Investigations & Expert Analysis:

○ Ability to assist customers in responding rapidly and effectively to computer-related incidents and should consistently exceed expectations while working in a customer-facing setting.

○ Lead complex, large-scale digital forensic investigations in sophisticated security breaches, determining initial attack vectors, scope of compromise, and data exfiltration.

○ Act as the lead technical subject matter expert in at least two of the following domains: host-based forensics (Windows, Linux, macOS), network forensics and full packet capture analysis, memory forensics, mobile device forensics (iOS/Android), or Cloud Forensics.

○ Maintain forensic integrity of evidence and produce comprehensive, court-admissible reports and deliverables for clients, legal counsel, and regulatory bodies.


● Proactive Resilience Engagements:

○ Conduct formal Incident Response Capability Assessments (IRCA), benchmarking client programs against maturity models to identify critical gaps in people, process, and technology and delivering strategic improvement roadmaps.

○ Lead and deliver Incident Response Plan Development (IRPD) engagements, creating comprehensive, actionable IR plans for clients based on NIST, SANS, and other industry-standard frameworks.

○ Serve as a trusted strategic advisor to client leadership (including C-suite and Board level) on matters of cyber risk, incident preparedness, and resilience strategy.

○ Design, develop, and facilitate a range of sophisticated breach simulation exercises, from executive-level, discussion-based tabletop exercises to immersive, technical simulations.

○ Create realistic, tailored threat scenarios (e.g. multi-stage ransomware, insider threat, nation-state espionage) that effectively test a client's decision-making, communication, and technical response functions under pressure.

○ Produce detailed post-exercise reports with actionable recommendations to drive continuous improvement in client security posture.


You'll need to have:

● Bachelor's degree or relevant work experience.

● Experience in the tools, techniques, and methodologies surrounding incident response, computer forensics, industrial control; systems, IoT, and eDiscovery.

● Experience in at least two (2) of the following operating systems: Windows, Linux, Unix, MacOS.

● Experience in consulting customers in DFIR capacity

● Experience managing the day-to-day aspects of protected customer relationships, as well as IT investigative cases and corporate security incidents.

● Ability to work in a dynamic environment, while maintaining confidentiality, and a professional image and approach with customers is critical.

● Willingness to travel on short notice.

● Fluency in English both written and verbal.

● Valid driver's license.


Even better if you have one or more of the following:

● Fluency in additional European languages (e.g., French, German, Dutch, etc.)

● Advanced degree in Computer Science, Digital Forensics, or Cyber Incident Response.

● Experience conducting security assessments, penetration testing, and ethical hacking.

● Experience in evidentiary procedures, volatile criminal/civil situations, fraud analysis and IR fundamentals.

● Understanding of information security; network architecture; general database concepts; document management; hardware and software troubleshooting; email systems, such as Microsoft Exchange, Office365, GSuite; Microsoft Office applications; and computer forensic tools such as EnCase and FTK, Velociraptor, EDR, NDR or XDR solutions -ie. CrowdStrike, Defender, SentinelOne, Darktrace, etc .

● Experience regarding malware analysis and reverse engineering, network forensics, memory forensics and mobile forensics. ● Experience in electronic crimes law enforcement, military intelligence, or with a security professional services organization. ● Experience in a security professional services consulting firm.

● One or more of the following professional certifications: GCFA, GCFE, GCIH, GNFA, CCSP, GREM, CISSP, CISM or equivalent, or ability to successfully achieve one within the first 6 months of employment.

● Experience as a Payment Card Industry (PCI) Forensic Investigator (PFI) or Qualified Security Assessor (QSA)

descriptionHtml:
Vacature: Security Officer

Semmie is een snelgroeiende vermogensbeheerder met een eigen digitaal platform die de vermogensbeheerwereld slimmer, transparanter en klantgerichter maakt. In een sterk gereguleerde sector is veiligheid cruciaal. Daarom zoeken wij een deskundige Security Officer die onze informatiebeveiliging en compliance naar een hoger niveau tilt. Wil jij bij een ambitieuze scale-up echt impact maken met jouw expertise? Lees dan snel verder.


Waarom jij belangrijk bent voor Semmie


Als Security Officer ben je verantwoordelijk voor het ontwikkelen, uitvoeren en bewaken van het informatiebeveiligingsbeleid binnen onze organisatie. Je werkt nauw samen met IT, Risk, Legal en Compliance om risico’s te identificeren, mitigeren en rapporteren, met inachtneming van wet- en regelgeving DORA en AVG.


Dit is wat je bij ons gaat doen

  • Onderhouden en verbeteren van het ISMS (Information Security Management System).
  • Bewaken van compliance met o.a. DORA, ISO 27001 en AVG.
  • Coördineren van risicoanalyses, audits, pen-tests en incidentrespons.
  • Onderhouden en actualiseren van security policies, awarenessprogramma’s, processen en controls.
  • Adviseren van directie en stakeholders over dreigingen, maatregelen en risico’s.
  • Aanspreekpunt voor toezichthouders en externe auditors op het gebied van informatiebeveiliging.
  • Samenwerken met DevOps en cloudbeveiligingsteams voor een veilige IT-architectuur (Azure).
  • De organisatie bewust om laten gaan met informatiebeveiligingrisico’s
  • Het organiseren en uitvoeren van testen van het Business Continuiteitsplan
  • Vanuit jouw informatiebeveiliging en privacy expertise bijdrage projecten.
  • Contact onderhouden met derde partijen over informatiebeveiliging en privacy.


Wie ben jij?

  • Je hebt hbo/wo werk- en denkniveau, bij voorkeur in IT, cybersecurity, bedrijfskunde of (technische) informatica.
  • Minimaal 3 jaar ervaring in informatiebeveiliging of security management binnen de financiële of tech-sector.
  • Kennis van relevante normen en wetgeving: ISO 27001, DORA en AVG.
  • Je bent nieuwsgierig en leert graag verder in jouw vakgebied.
  • Ervaring met cloud security en DevSecOps is een sterke plus.
  • Je bent analytisch, communicatief sterk, en weet complexe materie helder over te brengen.


Wat bieden wij?

  • Een baan voor 32 – 40 uur per week.
  • Een marktconform salaris, afhankelijk van ervaring (€ 4.000 - € 5.000 bruto per maand).
  • Aantrekkelijke secundaire arbeidsvoorwaarden, zoals het volgen van opleidingen.
  • Hybride werken met flexibele werktijden.
  • 25 vakantiedagen + 8% vakantiegeld + een pensioenregeling.
  • Een gezellige cultuur met vrijdagmiddagborrels en uitjes.
  • Een innovatieve, schaalbare omgeving waar je écht impact maakt.
  • Mogelijkheden voor certificering, training en doorgroei binnen security of compliance.


Ben je geïnteresseerd in deze leuke baan bij Semmie? Stuur dan een mail naar werken@semmie.nl. Laat in je motivatie weten wat jou geschikt maakt en waarom je bij Semmie wilt werken.


Acquisitie naar aanleiding van deze vacature wordt niet op prijs gesteld.

IAM Process Analyst – (Remote Contract)

We are seeking a detail-oriented professional to join our client's Identity & Access Management (IAM) function, reporting directly to the Head of IAM. This role will focus on reviewing, clarifying, and improving process documentation within the team.


Key responsibilities include:

  • Interviewing IAM team members to capture and understand technical processes
  • Reviewing existing process documentation and identifying gaps or inconsistencies
  • Updating and improving documentation to ensure accuracy, clarity, and completeness
  • Highlighting areas where processes can be better defined or streamlined


What we’re looking for:

  • Strong communication and analytical skills, with the ability to ask the right questions and extract key information
  • Experience working with technical teams and processes (IAM or related areas ideal)
  • Familiarity with Entra ID is helpful but not essential
  • Proactive, detail-focused, and capable of working independently in a remote setting


About the Organisation

You’ll be joining a global leader in engineering and technology, with operations in over 30 countries and a team of 15,000+ people.


This is a fully remote contract role and an excellent opportunity to support a global IAM function in strengthening its processes and documentation.


Reach out to Luke Finn on +44 203 053 3723 / luke@next-ventures.com
