descriptionHtml:
Ben jij een Netwerk Security Engineer met passie voor het beveiligen en segmenteren van complexe netwerken? Heb je ervaring met firewallbeheer en wil je je expertise inzetten bij uitdagende projecten bij toonaangevende klanten? Dan is dit jouw kans om als zelfstandig ondernemer impact te maken binnen het collectief van The Future Group (TFG).


Wat ga je doen?

Als Netwerk Security Engineer speel je een cruciale rol in het ontwerpen, implementeren en beheren van veilige netwerkoplossingen. Je bent veelgevraagd voor segmentatieprojecten en firewallbeheer en helpt organisaties hun netwerken te beschermen tegen steeds geavanceerdere dreigingen.


Concreet houd je je bezig met:

  • Ontwerpen en uitvoeren van netwerksegmentatieprojecten
  • Optimalisatie van firewalls (Palo Alto, Fortinet, Cisco)
  • Inrichten van VPN, IDS/IPS en netwerksecurity policies
  • Troubleshooting, monitoring en incident response binnen netwerkbeveiliging
  • Adviseren van klanten over best practices, architectuur en security by design


Wie ben jij?

  • Hbo-/wo-achtergrond in Informatica, Cyber Security of vergelijkbaar
  • Minimaal 4 jaar ervaring als Network / Security Engineer
  • Praktijkervaring met firewallbeheer (Palo Alto, Fortinet, Cisco)
  • Ervaring met netwerksegmentatie, routing, switching en VPN-oplossingen
  • Bekend met securityframeworks en compliance (ISO 27001, NIS2)
  • Certificeringen zoals PCNSE, NSE, CCNP Security of vergelijkbaar zijn een pré
  • Je bent pragmatisch, analytisch en communicatief sterk – je weet techniek te vertalen naar de business


Over The Future Group – Independent Together

Wij zijn geen broker. Geen detacheerder. Geen traditionele werkgever.

Wij zijn een collectief van meer dan 300 zelfstandige IT-professionals die vakmanschap combineren met vrijheid en samenwerking.

Dankzij onze unieke maatschap-structuur werk je niet als individuele ZZP’er, maar als partner binnen een collectief. Zo werk je veilig, zonder zorgen over de Wet DBA, met een goedgekeurde modelovereenkomst van de Belastingdienst.


👉 Ben jij de Netwerk Security Engineer die we zoeken? Stuur een mail met je CV naar anouk.vanmelis@the-future-group.com en ontdek hoe ondernemerschap, vrijheid en vakmanschap samenkomen bij The Future Group.

descriptionHtml:
Functieomschrijving

In de rol van Accountmanager (junior/medior) ben je verantwoordelijk voor het genereren van new business maar nog meer voor het managen van onze bestaande klantrelaties. Op basis van je eigen netwerk, het salesjaarplan of naar aanleiding van andere actuele aanknopingspunten benader je actief potentiële prospects en klanten binnen het zakelijke segment. Je signaleert specifieke klantwensen door te luisteren maar ook door te adviseren op basis van noodzaak en ervaringen. Als gelijkwaardige gesprekspartner achterhaal je de vraag achter de vraag en lever je inhoudelijk advies en concrete oplossingen doormiddel van de dienstverleningsmogelijkheden van Crisicom. Je werkt in nauw overleg samen met je directe collega’s van het salesteam, maar ook met de collega’s van de afdeling planning en cursusadministratie om onze klanten een kwalitatief hoogwaardige service te verlenen op het gebied van opleidingen, oefeningen en producten in relatie tot bedrijfshulpverlening en crisismanagement. Het salesteam bestaat uit twee salessupportmedewerkers, een sales- en marketingmedewerker, een accountmanager en manager

companyDescription: Bol is the largest online retailer in the Netherlands and Belgium. In 1999 we opened our virtual doors as first online bookstore in the Netherlands. Nowadays we’re working with 2.000 colleagues to deliver the best shopping platform ever. To be able to do this we have expanded our shopping platform in the 20 years of our existence. 2010 was the beginning of bol.com in Belgium. The first step towards our southern neighbors. And we opened our shopping platform to other retailers, for our customers to be able to find ‘everything’ on bol.com. Since 2016 it is possible for our partners to advertise via bol.com. And in 2017 Huub Vermeulen opened the doors of our bol fulfilment center. Shortly: together with 2.000 bol.commers we work to make the daily lives of our customer easier.
descriptionHtml:
How you make our customers happy?

By ensuring our platform provides a secure and near-frictionless experience for 13.7 million customers and 47,000 partners. As Security Lead SecOps, you combine robust defenses with business velocity, enabling teams to innovate quickly while maintaining exemplary security standards. You’ll transform complex security challenges into actionable strategies that protect customer data, partner assets, and platform integrity. Your leadership turns security into a business enabler rather than a blocker or, worse, an afterthought. As such, you directly enhance the trust that makes bol the preferred platform for millions.

The biggest challenge

Maintaining security excellence in a rapidly evolving tech environment where innovation never stops and isn’t intended to. Success calls for explaining highly technical security risks in plain language that resonates with stakeholders at all levels. How do you foster a security-first mindset without becoming the department of “no”? How do you empower autonomous teams to truly own security challenges while ensuring consistent standards? The key lies in building collaborative relationships, implementing smart automation, and knowing exactly when to gently nudge versus when to decisively intervene.

What You Do As Security Lead

As a captain of the SecOps team, you’ll initiate and drive security initiatives that protect our platform without impeding either business growth or innovation velocity. Working alongside security engineers, IT professionals, and business leaders, you’ll manage security operations that effortlessly scale with our ambitions. You’ll translate security risks into prioritized action plans, ensuring alignment between security objectives and business goals. From refining incident response protocols to advancing security automation, you’ll optimize how we detect, respond to, and prevent threats. Your ability to cut through complexity and focus on what matters most will be essential in maintaining our security posture without hampering innovation.

Role

A crucial part of your role involves proactive stakeholder management – keeping leadership informed without overwhelming them with technical details and helping engineering teams understand security implications without dictating solutions. You’ll champion security best practices while respecting team autonomy, cultivating an environment where security isn’t ‘just’ on everyone’s checklist but is a shared philosophy. In brief:

  • Lead security initiatives that balance protection with innovation enablement
  • Develop and maintain incident response protocols that minimize business impact
  • Transform security risks into prioritized action plans with clear ownership
  • Build strong relationships with engineering, product, and business stakeholders
  • Drive automation initiatives that enhance security coverage and efficiency
  • Foster a security-first culture through education and engagement
  • Coordinate cross-team security efforts and safeguard standards
  • Communicate security status and risks effectively to senior leadership

Why you can make a difference

You combine security expertise with proven project management capabilities. With 3+ years of experience in security operations and/or leadership roles, you master both the technical and organizational aspects of security at scale. Your structured approach to problem-solving enables you to break down complex security challenges into manageable components. While technically adept, you shine at stakeholder management – you know how to align security initiatives with business objectives and maintain appropriate guardrails. Experience with cloud environments and security monitoring tools is essential, as is staying current with emerging threats and industry trends.

3 reasons why this is (not) for you Switch to find out

  • - Ivory tower architect You prefer theoretical security models to hands-on operational challenges and real-world trade-offs
  • - Solo security superhero You'd rather single-handedly save the day than teach teams how to ingrain security into their DNA
  • - Binary thinker Something is either 100% secure or completely vulnerable - nuance and risk management aren't in your vocabulary
  • + Security enabler You consider security a primary business enabler, not a necessary evil - your mission is making innovation safer, not slower
  • + Goal-sett(l)er You excel at bringing diverse teams together around shared security ambitions while respecting their autonomy and expertise; you set the goals and let the teams settle on how to achieve them
  • + Chaos conqueror You transform chaotic security incidents into clear action plans, keeping cool when others might miss a beat

This is where you’ll work

You’ll join our SecOps team at the heart of bol’s security operations. Here, ethical hackers, defenders, and problem-solvers work together to protect one of the leading retail tech platforms in the Netherlands and Belgium. The setting is both challenging and supportive – we take security seriously but remain aware that security serves a greater function. We value collaboration over rigid controls, automation over manual processes, and pragmatic solutions over security theater. With 2,900 colleagues serving 13.7 million customers and 47,000 partners, your work has real impact. We foster continuous learning because, in security, standing still means falling behind. Potentially into oblivion. Want to help us stay secure while maintaining our innovation speed? Let’s discuss how you’ll contribute to our security journey!

We take pride in our B Corp certification and strive for continuous improvement every day. Our annual bonus is tied to sustainability goals, and we are committed to equality and equal opportunities for all.

companyDescription:
Adyen (AMS: ADYEN) is the financial technology platform of choice for leading companies. By providing end-to-end payments capabilities, data-driven insights, and financial products in a single global solution, Adyen helps businesses achieve their ambitions faster. With offices around the world, Adyen works with the likes of Meta, Uber, H&M, eBay, and Microsoft.

For support visit: https://support.adyen.com
companySlogan: Meet the financial technology platform helping the world’s leading businesses achieve their ambitions faster.
descriptionHtml:
This is Adyen

Adyen provides payments, data, and financial products in a single solution for customers like Meta, Uber, H&M, and Microsoft - making us the financial technology platform of choice. At Adyen, everything we do is engineered for ambition.

For our teams, we create an environment with opportunities for our people to succeed, backed by the culture and support to ensure they are enabled to truly own their careers. We are motivated individuals who tackle unique technical challenges at scale and solve them as a team. Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.

Security Detection and Monitoring Engineer

As a driven and proactive Security Detection and Monitoring Engineer you will focus on Security Operations, Detection Engineering, Threat Detection and Incident Response. You will develop and maintain security solutions to improve detection capabilities within the whole Adyen ecosystem, automate difficult manual tasks and investigate security incidents. In short, we want you to detect everything worth detecting, and then deal with it!

How do we do that?

  • Collaborating- To create meaningful detections and to respond to triggered alerts you will collaborate with other members of the security, product, development and infrastructure teams. We do not work in isolation.
  • Developing our very own tool- You will implement complex detection logic in our in-house developed security monitoring tool with a mix of Python and your bright ideas. Advanced Python development skills are a must, “a bit of scripting” is not enough for the challenges we have ahead of us.
  • Using our very own tool- all our security engineers developing new detections also act as security analysts and incident responders dealing with the alerts triggered by the monitoring system. We do not believe in the typical task segregation of traditional security operation centers. Do you agree with this view? Tell us why - or challenge us - in your motivation letter!

What You’ll Do

  • Create scalable and actionable detection logic for emerging threats in Python
  • Develop new solutions to hunt, detect and deal with threats
  • Operate and expand our in-house built security event monitoring tool
  • Save your colleague’s time and brainpower through automation
  • Maintain up to date knowledge of tools, techniques, and procedures used by attackers
  • Build and own security monitoring tooling in Adyen
  • Join the Security Duty group rotation

Who You Are

  • You have 3+ years of experience in one or more of the following security domains- Intrusion Detection, Detection Engineering, Threat Detection, Incident Response.
  • You are proficient in one or more of the following areas- Operating System security (Linux/MacOS), Container Security, Network security, SaaS/IaaS Security.
  • You understand security detection and monitoring without being limited to specific SIEM tools or vendors.
  • You have solid and demonstrable experience with Python, and are able to translate complex detection logic into advanced code with ease.
  • You are a builder at heart, and have the mindset to script, automate, and scope projects to avoid repetitive tasks.
  • You believe that the security of systems, people and processes requires a pragmatic and humane approach where communication is key

Our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion commitments

Our unique approach is a product of our diverse perspectives. This diversity of backgrounds and cultures is essential in helping us maintain our momentum. Our business and technical challenges are unique, and we need as many different voices as possible to join us in solving them - voices like yours. No matter who you are or where you’re from, we welcome you to be your true self at Adyen.

Studies show that women and members of underrepresented communities apply for jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. Does this sound like you? If so, Adyen encourages you to reconsider and apply. We look forward to your application!

What’s next?

Ensuring a smooth and enjoyable candidate experience is critical for us. We aim to get back to you regarding your application within 5 business days. Our interview process tends to take about 4 weeks to complete, but may fluctuate depending on the role. Learn more about our hiring process here. Don’t be afraid to let us know if you need more flexibility.

This role is based out of our Amsterdam office. We are an office-first company and value in-person collaboration; we do not offer remote-only roles.

companyDescription:
We deliver open source to the world faster,  more securely and more cost effectively than any other company.

We're also the publishers of Ubuntu, the world’s most popular enterprise Linux from cloud to edge, together with a passionate global community of 200,000+ contributors. 

Ubuntu means 'humanity to others'​. We chose it because it embodies the generosity at the heart of open source, the new normal for platforms and innovation.

Together with a community of 200,000, we publish an operating system that runs from the tiny connected devices up to the world's biggest mainframes, the platform that everybody uses on the public cloud, and the workstation experience of the world's most productive developers.

Secure and reliable, elegant and intuitive, and open for innovation - this is the future of open source, which is why we're proud to be the developers of the fastest growing Linux in the world despite already being the most widely deployed.

If you're interested in a career at Canonical, we are a remote-first company so please apply to any suitable role as skills are valued more than location, despite some having a preferred geographic preference. 

www.canonical.com
companySlogan: Enterprise open source, secured and delivered by the publisher of Ubuntu.
descriptionHtml:
This global leadership role in cyber security is to manage the Security Operations (SecOps) team responsible for design, implementation and evolution of Canonical security practices, techniques, tools, systems and policies. The team is the primary owner of strategy and practices that determine how Canonical secures its data, internal infrastructure and build processes. They are responsible for assuring the security and integrity of our own infrastructure and product deployments. They design and implement technical security controls that ensure security threats are automatically identified, contained and remediated. The team will also contribute ideas and requirements for Canonical product security, improving the resilience and robustness of all Ubuntu customers and users subject to cyber attack.

As a leader on cyber security in the company, the SecOps team manager will collaborate with our Organisational Learning and Development team to develop playbooks and facilitate SecOps training across Canonical. They will operate in a wider security organisation, run a high performing security team and improve Canonical's security posture. They will lead initiatives to integrate the team's insights into Canonical's broader software development process.

While this is a management position, we expect managers to be expert practitioners, able to lead by example, contribute at the highest level, and assess work based on their own professional experience and skill. Candidates should have deep, hands-on expertise with a range of open source and proprietary security tooling and practices, which they can integrate into a holistic next generation security solution across the breadth of Canonical's interests.

The SecOps team's mission is not only to secure Canonical, but also to contribute to the security of the wider open source ecosystem. They might share knowledge through public presentations and industry events, and share threat intelligence with the wider community or represent Canonical in sector-specific governance bodies.

This role reports to the CISO.

What you will do in this role:

  • Hire and mentor a team of outstanding technical security professionals
  • Define Canonical's SecOps security standards and playbooks
  • Own and drive the architecture and design of the SOC
  • Analyse and improve Canonical's security architecture
  • Evaluate, select and implement new security tools and practices
  • Identify, contain and guide the remediation of security threats and cyber attacks
  • Grow the presence and thought leadership of Canonical SecOps practice
  • Contribute to open source threat intelligence initiatives
  • Drive threat modelling, table top exercises and other SecOps practices across Engineering, IS and Canonical
  • Develop Canonical SecOps learning and development materials
  • Publish blog posts, whitepapers and conference presentations
  • Identify, implement and track SecOps KPIs
  • Plan and deliver SecOps work in the framework of Canonical's agile engineering practice
  • Work with Security leadership to present information and influence change

What we are looking for

  • Proven track record of mitigating with advanced threat actors and nation state threats
  • Expert technical understanding of SOCs from the ground up
  • In depth knowledge of SOC architecture and design including strategies for logging, firewalls, network segmentation, honeypots etc
  • Someone who understands how the SOC works not just how to use it
  • Expert in Linux security
  • Ability to define, implement, automate and measure effective incident response playbooks
  • Knowledge of security architecture and market-leading security tools
  • Experience contributing to, and consuming, threat intelligence feeds
  • Experience in security risk management frameworks such as NIST CSF
  • An exceptional academic track record from both high school and university
  • Undergraduate degree in Computer Science or STEM, or a compelling narrative about your alternative path
  • Drive and a track record of going above-and-beyond expectations
  • Deep personal motivation to be at the forefront of technology security
  • Leadership and management ability
  • Excellent business English writing and presentation skills
  • Confidence to report security performance metrics with accountability for accuracy and completeness

Optional things we value

  • Experience in offensive or defensive security teams with hands-on ability
  • Experience with open source security tools
  • Experience with security standards such as ISO 27001
  • Experience with security posture management of corporate endpoitns

descriptionHtml:
Preventing detecting and responding to risks like commercial and cybercrime, litigation and improving integrity within organizations is all about using technology smarter, whether through digital forensics or the latest eDiscovery methodologies. Our clients look to us to make it happen, and as a Forensic Technology specialist you’ll be right at the heart of that goal. It’s a role with far-reaching implications, so you’ll be working with stakeholders across industries with some of the most leading technology, deep subject-matter knowledge, and broad global sector experience.

The opportunity - What future are you building towards?

Forensic & Integrity Services (F&IS, Forensics) is a rapidly growing specialty within our organization. Our Forensics practice advises organisations and their (outside) legal counsel on complex business matters that involve financial, fraud and digital forensic investigations and economic damages arising from disputes on various matters.

The Forensic & Integrity Services team is a fast-growing group of consultants with different (study) backgrounds. The team is young, collegial, informal and there is plenty of room to grow within the organization. Our dynamic and talented Forensic Technology team, a specialty group within the Forensics practice, is currently seeking the right technical candidate for a career opportunity as a (senior) consultant level position.

Our team - Do great minds always think alike?

Our team comprises of Forensic Technology specialists who capture, consolidate, interrogate and analyse large volumes of structured and unstructured data, conduct post cyber-incident investigations and perform post-breach (privacy) impact assessments for clients all over Europe and the rest of the world.

Responsibilities – How can you make a lasting impact in a rapidly changing world?

As part of the team, you will allow our clients and their legal counsel to make informed, strategic decisions, by providing them with Digital Forensic, eDiscovery- and (Cyber) Incident & Privacy Response related services.

You will have a (technical) contribution to F&IS client engagements and internal projects. An important part of your role will be actively participating as a team member by sharing responsibility, providing support, maintaining communication, and updating senior team members on progress while deepening your subject matter knowledge and developing valuable insights. You will assist in performing (forensic) analysis and preparing reports and schedules that will be delivered to clients and other parties.

As an EY employee, you will build strong internal relationships within consulting and across other services. As part of our global network of over 4.000 forensic and technology professionals working in multi-disciplinary teams, you will gain valuable experience across industries and geographies.

Job requirements – Where do you start if you want to change the world?

  • A bachelor’s or master’s degree in the area of (Forensic) information technology (IT), Cybersecurity, Business IT, Computer Science or any another relevant study in this field.
  • 1-4 years of experience in an (forensic) IT environment, preferably including programming and relational database design and management.
  • Experience with computer forensics applications such as EnCase, FTK, Magnet Axiom or HELIX.
  • Experience with document review platforms including Relativity, NUIX, Reveal.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, an inquisitive mindset, high level of attention for detail and an affinity with (forensic) IT.
  • Excellent communication skills and excellent command of the English language, both written and spoken.
  • The drive to continuously improve your own work and that of our clients, we are looking for people who are innovative, agile, and flexible in a rapidly changing world.
  • An inclusive mindset. We are looking for people who seek and embrace different perspectives, value differences, and work together inclusively to build safety and trust.

What we offer – Is everything that counts being counted?

We offer you an attractive employment package that rewards individual and team achievements:

    • Vitality: the choice is yours. An online session with an external vitality coach, practical tips on topics such as nutrition, energy, sleep and mindset, a discount at Healthcity and/or a voucher card to rent a padel court.
    • Flexible working: you decide where and when you work in consultation with your team. At home, at the office, at the client or from another European country (up to 20 working days per year).
    • Home office arrangement: to optimize your home office, EY provides the following resources for you: office chair, sit/stand desk, an external monitor, a desk lamp, a (Bluetooth) headset, and a large external keyboard.
    • Birth leave: EY will pay 100% of the birth leave, in the case of an additional birth leave, EY will pay 70% of the salary.
For a full overview of our employment package click here: Hoe het is om bij ons te werken | EY – Global | EY - Nederland

The Office

Our office in Amsterdam is located in the Zuidas, a bustling area where many businesses are situated. In our Cross Towers, Anouchka and Lucia welcome up to 1.000 people every day upon arrival. Haven't had breakfast yet? Taib will make his special smoothie for you or simply a delicious cappuccino. During the lunch break, the team of Hans and Mehdi will be ready with a fresh lunch for you. For a breathtaking view of Amsterdam, head to the 22nd floor. Collaborating takes on a new meaning with the Wavespace. This high-tech environment brings clients and colleagues together virtually from all over the world!

Recruitment procedure – How can you prepare for a long life together?

the selection procedure exists out of 2 interview rounds and an assessment

Additional option: (After we have received and selected your CV, a short telephone introduction will follow. Then you make an online assessment in your own time. After completing the assessment, we will schedule your first interview, which will take place with the Recruiter and someone from the team. In case of a positive outcome of the first interview, a second (and also last) interview will be scheduled with other members of the team.)

Due to laws and regulations, and because EY (and in some cases its clients) has high demands on the reliability and integrity of its employees, every new employee will be screened. Depending on your rank the pre-employment screening could contain the following: Certificate of Conduct (in Dutch: VOG) or - if requesting a Dutch VOG is not possible - Criminal Background Check of the country you are currently living or have lived during the last five years, integrity questionnaire, verification of ID, diploma’s and/or work experience and consultation of the Dutch insolvency registry.

descriptionHtml:
Would you like to work as an Information Security Officer at a European education powerhouse that is transforming classrooms every day?


Are you ready to shape and lead the security strategy in an international environment where millions of students and teachers rely on secure digital learning solutions?


For our client in the educational technology sector, we are looking for an:


Information Security Officer


Founded in 2010 by a group of entrepreneurs with a background in educational publishing and technology, this organization’s mission is to make learning more accessible, secure, and impactful across Europe. Today, more than 400 employees work across multiple countries, delivering digital solutions that are used daily by schools, universities, and corporate organizations. What sets this company apart is its real impact in classrooms: students and teachers use their platforms during lessons, assessments, and learning journeys.


The organization combines the legacy of educational publishing with the power of modern technology. From printed textbooks to fully digital-first solutions, they have successfully transitioned into a provider of scalable and secure learning platforms. Their products range from digital course materials and adaptive learning platforms to analytics tools. The technical environment is centered around Microsoft Azure, infrastructure security, and secure software development practices. The culture is best described as “hands-on and collaborative”: engineers and security specialists work closely together, hackathons are organized regularly, and developers are challenged to embed security early in their code.


As an Information Security Officer, you will be at the heart of the organization’s security strategy. You will be responsible for protecting systems, applications, and data, while ensuring compliance with international standards such as ISO 27001 and GDPR. Your role will cover incident response, vulnerability management, and secure coding practices. It is a role that is both strategic and hands-on: you will advise management on risks and governance while directly engaging with developers and engineers to embed security into daily operations.


The profile of the Information Security Officer:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in IT, Computer Science, or Information Security (or equivalent)
  • At least 5 years of experience in cybersecurity (for medior position)
  • At least 10 years of experience in cybersecurity (for senior level)
  • Experience in both offensive and defensive security
  • Knowledge of MITRE ATT&CK, OWASP, CIS, and risk assessment methodologies
  • Expertise in cloud security (Azure), IAM, and secure software development
  • Familiarity with SIEM, EDR, vulnerability management tools, and penetration testing
  • Certifications such as CISSP, OSCP, CISM, CEH, or GIAC are highly desirable
  • Strong communication and analytical skills, able to stand your ground in discussions with developers
  • Fluent in English


Package deal:

  • Salary up to €6,000 gross per month (medior) and up to €7,000 (senior), based on 40 hours
  • Paid vacation days
  • Pension scheme
  • Travel allowance
  • Laptop and phone
  • Fixed workplace
  • Flexible working hours
  • Work-from-home options
  • Training budget
  • Career growth opportunities
  • Bonus scheme
  • Extras such as team events and hackathons


Interested?

Send your CV to gokhanaytekin@sterrk.nl. Let’s have a cup of coffee soon!

It is a role that is both strategic and hands-on: you will advise management on risks and governance while directly engaging with developers and engineers to embed security into daily operations.The profile of the Information Security Officer:Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in IT, Computer Science, or Information Security (or equivalent)At least 5 years of experience in cybersecurity (for medior position)At least 10 years of experience in cybersecurity (for senior level)Experience in both offensive and defensive securityKnowledge of MITRE ATT&CK, OWASP, CIS, and risk assessment methodologiesExpertise in cloud security (Azure), IAM, and secure software developmentFamiliarity with SIEM, EDR, vulnerability management tools, and penetration testingCertifications such as CISSP, OSCP, CISM, CEH, or GIAC are highly desirableStrong communication and analytical skills, able to stand your ground in discussions with developersFluent in EnglishPackage deal:Salary up to €6,000 gross per month (medior) and up to €7,000 (senior), based on 40 hoursPaid vacation daysPension schemeTravel allowanceLaptop and phoneFixed workplaceFlexible working hoursWork-from-home optionsTraining budgetCareer growth opportunitiesBonus schemeExtras such as team events and hackathonsInterested?Send your CV to gokhanaytekin@sterrk.nl. Let’s have a cup of coffee soon!

descriptionHtml:
About FareHarbor

At FareHarbor, our mission is to make experiences better for everyone. Founded in 2013 in Hawaii and acquired by Booking Holdings in 2018, FareHarbor creates powerful tools that enable our clients (think boat rentals, museums, food tours, events and more!) to operate and grow.

With over 20,000 clients across 90+ countries—we’re the largest in our industry and shaping the future of travel, together.

Our team is an ‘Ohana of 700+ people around the world. We’re passionate about pioneering an industry, embracing challenges with open arms, and delivering value to the experiences industry.

FareHarbor Core Values

  • Think Client First
  • We Are One ‘Ohana
  • Be Curious and Learn
  • Own It.
  • Act With Integrity
  • Embrace the Challenge

Why FareHarbor?

Founding FareHarbor required unwavering passion. Turning a start-up into the world’s leading and largest reservation software for tours, activities, and attractions required relentless dedication and vision. To date, we’ve helped over 20,000 global businesses operate successfully and are proud to have played a role in enabling business owners to live their dreams.

And since day one, we’ve known that our real success lies in our people—the Ohana.

With each new feature launched and new client onboarded, there is a team of incredible people behind the scenes who are full of dedication, passion, energy, and the will to succeed. We encourage everyone to bring their whole selves to work—to believe in their abilities, to freely express their creativity, and to contribute with their own uniqueness by wearing their true colors. We take care of one another and always prioritize health and wellbeing. We give our people the space and trust to learn, to try, to succeed, to collaborate, to think outside of the box, to make mistakes, and even to fail. And then we come together to try again.

From the minute you join, you have a voice. You find your space. You make an impact. We celebrate our victories, shout our successes, and are always eager to tackle new challenges. And we can’t wait to see all that’s to come.

About The Role

We’re looking for a hands-on Manager of our Security and IT teams to lead the tactical execution of our security program and oversee our internal IT support operations.

In this hybrid role, you’ll manage a small but high-impact IT service desk team to ensure employees have exceptional IT support. As a highly technical professional with adept expertise in one or more Cyber Security domains, you will also be responsible for leading a team of Security Engineers to design, build, deliver, and maintain robust security software, tools, and automation that protect FareHarbor’s assets and contribute to our organizational security posture.

This role requires a deep understanding of cybersecurity engineering principles, secure software development practices, and relevant technologies. Additionally, strong people leadership skills will be needed to drive team performance, development, and operational excellence in solution development.

The Manager, Security & IT will collaborate with product Managers, engineering teams, and security stakeholders to deliver the security roadmap and ensure the team's developed software and automation are effective, secure, and adhere to SLAs. The person in this role will balance project delivery with risk to make informed trade-off decisions, and maintain healthy runtime operations for software developed by the team.

As part of the Booking Holdings family of brands, much of our IT and Security programs overlap with our sister companies. This role requires strong collaboration skills and the ability to align with external stakeholders to be an effective bridge between brands. You’ll be instrumental in building Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) that clarify ownership between teams, reduce friction, and ensure both IT and security initiatives are well-coordinated both within FareHarbor, and with our parent company. This is a unique opportunity for someone who enjoys solving practical technical problems, driving process maturity, and managing a team across two vital functions.

What You’ll Do Here

  • Lead and develop the IT service desk team to deliver excellent technical support for all employees.
  • Lead and manage the security engineering team in implementing and maintaining security controls, tools, and monitoring practices.
  • Own the tactical execution of the company’s security program and operational support plan, including incident response, vulnerability management, and compliance support.
  • Partner with product Managers, engineering manager, sister brand’s security team and other stakeholders to ensure the delivery of the roadmap, identifying resourcing needs, technical scope and complexity, and critical risks and dependencies.
  • Oversee the design, implementation, delivery, and maintenance of robust security solutions, services, and capabilities that meet business objectives and customer value.
  • Ensure adherence to secure coding standards, testing practices, and quality requirements for all developed software.
  • Drive operational excellence and continuous improvement within the team by identifying opportunities for efficiency in security operations. Champion the adoption of emerging technologies (e.g., AI, Advanced Analytics, Automation) to enhance team-developed security tools and oversee the rollout of improved cybersecurity processes and engineering best practices related to secure software development.
  • Act as an Advanced Subject Matter Expert in relevant cybersecurity domains and secure software development. Provide technical leadership in evaluating security technologies, contributing to security architecture discussions for developed tools, and guiding the team on complex engineering challenges, including secure software design and implementation
  • Promote knowledge sharing and technical skill development within the cybersecurity teams
  • Create and refine Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) in collaboration with our parent company to define responsibilities and streamline workflows across IT and security.
  • Ensure timely resolution of IT tickets and escalation of critical issues, maintaining high levels of user satisfaction.
  • Oversee employee onboarding and offboarding processes, including access provisioning and hardware allocation.
  • Coordinate IT asset inventory, software licensing, and procurement.
  • Monitor and maintain endpoint security tools, SSO configurations, and mobile device management (MDM) systems.
  • Support internal audits, risk assessments, and policy documentation as part of the broader security compliance framework.
  • Stay current with evolving IT and security trends, driving improvements where appropriate.

Requirements

  • 5+ years of experience in IT operations, information security, or a related field, including 2+ years in a people management role.
  • Security Engineering (Domain Specific): Expertise as an Advanced Subject Matter Expert (SME), with a focus on domain-specific areas like Application Security, Cloud Security, Data security, Security Automation, or Threat Detection Engineering.
  • Strong technical foundation across IT infrastructure, endpoint management, identity and access management, and security best practices.
  • Demonstrated experience managing helpdesk/support teams and security professionals.
  • Proven ability to develop, document, and operationalize SOPs in collaboration with cross-functional or external teams.
  • Excellent problem-solving, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Familiarity with tools such as Google Workspace, Okta , MDM solutions , and ticketing systems (e.g., Jira, Zendesk).
  • Experience achieving and maintaining SOC 2 Type II certification, and adhering to NIST guidelines
  • Experience working in organizations with a parent/subsidiary structure is a plus.
  • Relevant certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISM, CompTIA Security+, ITIL) are a bonus but not required.

Benefits

  • Global leave benefit
    • 22 weeks paid parental leave
    • 2 weeks paid grandparent leave
    • Extended care and bereavement leave
    • Life insurance policy
  • Pension Plan
  • Central Amsterdam Location
  • Discount CZ insurance
  • Working in a multicultural environment - 45 different nationalities
  • Commuting allowance for public transport & subsidized lunch
  • Wellness benefits (Headspace subscription & wellness webinars)
  • Hybrid friendly
  • Work-from-home assistance
  • Educational Opportunities
  • Social hours & events and team-building
  • 26 vacation days per year
FareHarbor is committed to creating a diverse environment, and we are an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, or veteran status. We welcome talent that can offer us new insights and perspectives on challenges that we face, and we take measures to eliminate unconscious bias throughout the interview and hiring process. In tandem, we work to cultivate an inclusive culture in which all of our employees can be their authentic selves.

To learn more about how we use your information, see our Privacy Statement for Applicants. By submitting your application, you confirm that you understand and agree that your information will be processed in accordance with our Privacy Statement for Applicants.

Any offer of work (e.g. employment, assignment) will be subjected to the successful completion of pre-employment screening.

companyDescription: The Nebius AI Cloud brings powerful full-stack infrastructure for AI developers and practitioners across startups, enterprises and science institutes to build and deploy generative AI applications and rapidly deliver scientific breakthroughs by training and running ML models within a secure, high-performance, and cost-optimized cloud environment.
descriptionHtml:
Why work at Nebius


Nebius is leading a new era in cloud computing to serve the global AI economy. We create the tools and resources our customers need to solve real-world challenges and transform industries, without massive infrastructure costs or the need to build large in-house AI/ML teams. Our employees work at the cutting edge of AI cloud infrastructure alongside some of the most experienced and innovative leaders and engineers in the field.


Where we work


Headquartered in Amsterdam and listed on Nasdaq, Nebius has a global footprint with R&D hubs across Europe, North America, and Israel. The team of over 800 employees includes more than 400 highly skilled engineers with deep expertise across hardware and software engineering, as well as an in-house AI R&D team.


The role


We are looking for a Data Analyst to help us improve the reliability and efficiency of our cloud platform—across the hardware, compute, and managed services layers. In this role, you will work closely with engineers, product managers, SREs, and business stakeholders, driving insights and process improvements that ensure our infrastructure runs smoothly at scale. This is a highly cross-functional position where you’ll combine technical skills with strong analytical thinking and a collaborative mindset. If you thrive on solving complex data challenges while enabling better decision-making, we’d love to hear from you.


Your responsibilities will include:


  • Analyze platform reliability data across hardware, compute, and managed services layers.
  • Build dashboards and reporting solutions that provide visibility into processes, system health, and KPIs.
  • Collaborate with engineering, operations, and business teams to identify process inefficiencies and define improvements.
  • Design, develop, and maintain ETL/ELT pipelines and data transformation workflows.
  • Automate analysis and reporting tasks using Python and SQL.
  • Act as a bridge between technical and non-technical stakeholders, communicating insights effectively.
  • Conduct experiments and prepare results for internal and external reports.
  • Contribute to data-driven initiatives that increase platform availability, resiliency, and scalability.


We expect you to have


  • 4+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, Data Engineer, or in a related role.
  • Strong expertise in SQL and hands-on experience with Python for analysis and automation.
  • Experience designing and maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines and data workflows.
  • Proficiency with BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, or similar) to create impactful dashboards.
  • Solid understanding of statistics (e.g., hypothesis testing, regression, distributions) and how to apply them to real-world data problems.
  • Familiarity with Git for version control and Docker for container-based development.
  • Strong analytical skills: ability to translate complex data into actionable, business-relevant insights.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills for stakeholder collaboration.
  • A structured, independent work style and ability to prioritize in a fast-paced environment.
  • Working knowledge of spoken and written English.


It will be an added bonus if you have


  • Experience working in cloud environments (AWS, GCP, Azure, or similar).
  • Familiarity with cloud infrastructure monitoring and reliability tools (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana).
  • Understanding of risk management, stability engineering, or incident response processes.
  • Background in software development or automation beyond data processing.
  • Comfortable working with multiple teams in a cross-functional stakeholder environment.
  • Experience with container orchestration platforms such as Kubernetes.
  • Background in Mathematics or Economics.


What we offer


  • Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
  • Opportunities for professional growth within Nebius.
  • Hybrid working arrangements.
  • A dynamic and collaborative work environment that values initiative and innovation.


We’re growing and expanding our products every day. If you’re up to the challenge and are excited about AI and ML as much as we are, join us!

companyDescription:
JetBrains creates intelligent software development tools consistently used and trusted by 11.4 million professionals and 88 Fortune Global Top 100 companies. Our lineup of more than 30 products includes IDEs for most programming languages and technologies, such as IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, and others, as well as products for team collaboration, like YouTrack and TeamCity. JetBrains is also known for creating the Kotlin programming language, a cross-platform language used by more than 5 million developers worldwide yearly and recommended by Google as the preferred language for Android development. The company is headquartered in Prague, Czech Republic, and has offices around the world.

companySlogan: The Drive to Develop
descriptionHtml:
At JetBrains, we are passionate about creating software tools for individual developers and teams that help them work more productively and enjoyably.

Our Security team is currently looking for an experienced Security Operations Center (SOC) Specialist to join our growing team and help manage and enhance our monitoring and incident response capabilities. We are building a system to monitor infrastructure anomalies and are looking for a person to manage alerts, analyze incidents, and contribute to the continuous improvement of our monitoring systems and processes.

This role is a great opportunity to make a difference if you are passionate about data, monitoring, incident response, and developing resilient systems to safeguard infrastructure.


In this role, you will:

  • Take ownership of security management, monitoring, and investigation, and respond to infrastructure security alerts generated by our anomaly detection and monitoring systems.
  • Continuously review and refine alerting mechanisms to minimize noise and maximize actionable insights.
  • Assist in building and maintaining workflows, rules, and playbooks for the monitoring system to support incident response activities.
  • Aggregate and analyze infrastructure and application logs to detect anomalies and malicious actions.
  • Analyze security incidents and escalate, contain, and remediate issues as needed.
  • Write internal memos and articles about issues and related best practices.
  • Report incident highlights to the Security team.


We will be happy to have you on our team if you have:

  • Proven experience in a SOC, incident response, or infrastructure monitoring role.
  • Familiarity with log analysis tools, SIEM platforms, or monitoring solutions used for anomaly detection.
  • A strong understanding of security incident response processes, including triage, investigation, and action plans.
  • Knowledge of underlying infrastructure technologies (e.g. servers, networking, cloud environments) and their potential vulnerabilities.
  • Proficiency in scripting or automation tools (e.g. Python and Terraform) to streamline security processes.
  • Good written and spoken English communication skills.
  • A degree in computer science, information technology, or another relevant discipline.
  • A strong desire to learn more about cybersecurity and grow in this domain.
  • Analytical and problem-solving skills, with a results-oriented mindset.


We would be thrilled if you have:

  • Experience using or administering Splunk or any other SIEM platform.
  • Knowledge of cloud environments such as AWS, GCP, or Azure, and their native monitoring and security tools.
  • The ability to single-handedly find a compromised server on the network and investigate the security breach.
  • Broad knowledge of system administration.
  • Certifications such as AWS Certified Security – Specialty or GIAC Certified Incident Handler (GCIH).


We process the data provided in your job application in accordance with the Recruitment Privacy Policy (https://www.jetbrains.com/legal/privacy/privacy-recruitment.html)

companyDescription:
Who are we?

ASML is an innovation leader in the global semiconductor industry. We make machines that chipmakers use to mass produce microchips. Founded in 1984 in the Netherlands with just a handful of employees, we’ve now grown to over 40,000 employees, 143 nationalities and more than 60 locations around the world.

What do we do?

We provide chipmakers with hardware, software and services to mass produce patterns on silicon through lithography. Our lithography systems use ultraviolet light to create billions of tiny structures on silicon that together make up a microchip. We push our technology to new limits to enable our customers to create smaller, faster and more powerful chips.

Who are our people?

While you may think that only engineers and mathematicians work at ASML, you'll be surprised to find out that our people come from a wide variety of backgrounds. Across ASML, we have dedicated teams that manage customer support, communications and media, IT, software development and more. Every team in the company is essential for pushing our technology and the industry forward.

If you love to tackle challenges and innovate in a collaborative, supportive and inclusive environment with all the flexibility and freedom to unleash your full potential, ASML is the place to be.

Join us!
companySlogan: Changing the world, one nanometer at a time
descriptionHtml:
Introduction to the Job

As an Expert Security Analyst, you will take a leadership role within the Security Incident Response Team (SIRT).


Role and Responsibilities

Your primary focus will be on high-level security incident response (Cyber, IT, OT, DLP, Physical), threat hunting, and driving strategic security improvements. You will also play a key role in mentoring analysts and shaping the organization’s security posture.


This position requires deep technical expertise, strong analytical skills, and the ability to lead complex security investigations and security improvements.


The Security Incident Response Team (SIRT) operates within the Security Operations Center (SOC) to detect and mitigate security threats in real time. As an Expert security Analyst, you will be responsible for both operational security monitoring and security improvements.


In this role, you will:

  • Actively monitor security alerts for malicious activity or anomalies, ensuring swift response.
  • Lead investigations into high-profile, complex, or advanced persistent threats (APTs).
  • Proactively search for hidden threats and improve detection capabilities.
  • Correlate data across multiple sources to detect sophisticated attack patterns.
  • Develop advanced detection techniques and security automation strategies.
  • Act as an SME (subject matter expert) for SecOps tools and threat domains.
  • Provide guidance and mentorship to analysts at all levels.


Education and experience

We seek an experienced security expert with a strategic mindset and a proven ability to handle complex security challenges. The ideal candidate will have:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s in Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or related field.
  • 7+ years in advanced cybersecurity roles.
  • CISSP, GCIH, GCFA, CISM preferred.


Skills

  • Expertise in Security Monitoring, Log Analysis, and Threat Hunting.
  • Deep knowledge of a wide range of domains such as Endpoint, Network, OT, Information and Cloud Security.
  • Strong Leadership & Mentorship Capabilities.


Other Information

  • The role may involve shift work or on-call duties to provide 24/7 security coverage.
  • This position is primarily office-based, with the possibility of remote work.
  • It is preferred if you possess a valid work permit for the Netherlands.


This position requires access to controlled technology, as defined in the United States Export Administration Regulations (15 C.F.R. 730, et seq.). Qualified candidates must be legally authorized to access such controlled technology prior to beginning work. Business demands may require ASML to proceed with candidates who are immediately eligible to access controlled technology.


Diversity and inclusion

ASML is an Equal Opportunity Employer that values and respects the importance of a diverse and inclusive workforce. It is the policy of the company to recruit, hire, train and promote persons in all job titles without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, veteran status, disability, sexual orientation, or gender identity. We recognize that diversity and inclusion is a driving force in the success of our company.

companyDescription:
SBM Offshore is the world’s deepwater ocean-infrastructure expert. Through the design, construction, installation, and operation of offshore floating facilities, we play a pivotal role in a just transition. By advancing our core, we deliver cleaner, more efficient energy production. By pioneering more, we unlock new markets within the blue economy.
 
More than 7,800 SBMers collaborate worldwide to deliver innovative solutions as a responsible partner towards a sustainable future, balancing ocean protection with progress
companySlogan: True. Blue. Transition.
descriptionHtml:
Imagine your career taking you to the depths of innovation and the heights of impact. Our people enable continuous progress. Their commitment, collective expertise, and unique capabilities are the engine room behind SBM Offshore’s True. Blue. Transition. - shaping the future of energy, and beyond.

About Us:

SBM Offshore is the world’s deepwater ocean-infrastructure expert. Our work is already resulting in cleaner, more efficient energy production. True. Blue. Transition. is our promise to enable that into the future while at the same time using our expertise to support new and existing markets in the blue economy. It starts with Advancing our Core: continuing to advance the decarbonization of traditional energy production. While Pioneering More: helping to enable the energy transition and using our unique capabilities in ocean infrastructure to support more industries to grow sustainably. Sharing our experience for a better blue tomorrow.

Purpose


  • As an IT Corporate Security and Control Manager, you oversee and ensure the security, performance, and governance of the organization's IT infrastructure. Develop and implement strategies to protect systems and data, monitor IT performance, and enforce compliance with regulatory and internal standards. Lead teams in identifying risks, optimizing system performance, and enhancing control measures to safeguard the organization's technology assets.


Responsibilities


  • Lead and manage IT security initiatives: Oversee the development and implementation of robust IT security strategies and policies to safeguard the organization’s digital assets.
  • Ensure performance optimization: Monitor and enhance the performance of IT systems and infrastructure, ensuring optimal efficiency and minimal downtime.
  • Conduct security risk assessments: Identify and mitigate potential security risks, conducting regular audits and assessments to ensure compliance with industry standards and regulations.
  • Coordinate with cross-functional teams: Collaborate with other departments to integrate security measures into all aspects of IT projects and operations.
  • Develop and enforce control procedures: Establish and maintain control procedures to ensure the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of data and IT resources.
  • Manage incident response: Lead the response to security incidents, ensuring rapid and effective resolution to minimize impact on business operations.
  • Mentor and train team members: Provide guidance and training to IT staff on security protocols, best practices, and emerging threats.
  • Report to senior management: Provide regular updates to senior management on security performance, risks, and mitigation strategies, ensuring alignment with business objectives.


Education


  • Bachelor's degree in computer science, information technology, cybersecurity, or a related field is required.
  • Certifications such as CISSP (Certified Information Systems Security Professional), CISM (Certified Information Security Manager), or PMP (Project Management Professional) are preferred.
  • Master’s Degree in cybersecurity, information technology, business administration, or a related field is preferred but not mandatory.
  • Proficiency in English is essential for effective communication with team members, stakeholders, and external vendors.


Experience


  • Minimum of 10 years of experience in IT security, infrastructure management, or a related field, with at least 5 years in a leadership or managerial role.
  • Proven experience in developing, implementing, and overseeing IT security strategies, policies, and procedures to ensure the protection of organizational assets.
  • Strong technical background in designing, implementing, and managing security frameworks, risk management, and compliance with industry standards (such as ISO/IEC 27001).
  • Experience with IT service management (ITSM) frameworks, including incident management, change management, and problem management processes.
  • Knowledge of security operations tools and techniques, including SIEM (Security Information and Event Management), IDS/IPS (Intrusion Detection System/Intrusion Prevention System), and threat intelligence platforms.
  • Experience in auditing and compliance processes to ensure adherence to regulatory requirements and internal policies.
  • Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams, mentor technical staff, and collaborate effectively with stakeholders at all levels of the organization.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to manage complex security projects and present findings and recommendations to senior leadership.


Functional Competencies

Governance, Risk and Control

Analytics and reporting

Cost and budget control

Business Partnering

Risk Management

Systems and Technology

GENERAL INFORMATION

SBM Offshore N.V. is a listed holding company that is headquartered in Amsterdam. It holds direct and indirect interests in other companies that collectively with SBM Offshore N.V. form the SBM Offshore Group (“the Company”). For further information, please visit our website at www.sbmoffshore.com. The companies in which SBM Offshore N.V., directly and indirectly, owns investments are separate entities.

SBM Offshore is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. SBM Offshore does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, non-disqualifying physical or mental disability, national origin, veteran status, or any other basis covered by appropriate law. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need.

Jouw rol:

In deze veelzijdige functie ben jij een essentiële schakel in het logistieke proces binnen een industriële omgeving. Je zorgt ervoor dat alle laad- en loswerkzaamheden soepel verlopen en ondersteunt waar nodig de productie- en logistieke afdelingen. Dankzij jouw inzet blijven processen efficiënt, veilig en opgeruimd verlopen. Jij houdt van aanpakken, hebt geen 9-tot-5-mentaliteit en voelt je thuis in een dynamische en industriële werkomgeving.


Klinkt dit als jouw volgende stap? Lees dan verder!


Wat ga je doen?

  • Coördineren en uitvoeren van laad- en loswerkzaamheden voor schepen en vrachtwagens
  • Controleren van vrachtdocumenten en het bijhouden van logistieke administratie waar nodig
  • Ondersteunen van de productieafdeling bij interne logistieke taken
  • Schoonhouden van installaties, terreinen, silo’s en logistieke apparatuur
  • Zorgdragen voor een veilige werkomgeving en naleven van veiligheidsvoorschriften
  • Verplaatsen van goederen met behulp van heftrucks en andere interne transportmiddelen
  • Melden en registreren van afwijkingen of storingen
  • BHV-rol


Wat neem je mee?

  • LBO/MBO werk- en denkniveau
  • Geldig VCA-basis certificaat of bereidheid om deze te behalen
  • Geldig BHV-certificaat of bereidheid om deze te behalen
  • In het bezit van een geldig heftruckcertificaat
  • Goede beheersing van de Nederlandse taal
  • Basiskennis van Engels en/of Duits is een pré
  • Een flexibele en praktische werkhouding
  • Ervaring in een logistieke of industriële werkomgeving


Wat kun je verwachten?

  • Een bruto maandsalaris tussen € 2.295 en € 3.279 (afhankelijk van ervaring & dienstverband)
  • Een fulltime dienstverband bij de opdrachtgever, het gaat niet om een uitzendcontract
  • Een dynamische werkomgeving bij een duurzame en groeiende organisatie
  • Een hecht team van betrokken collega’s
  • Ruimte voor ontwikkeling en interne doorgroeimogelijkheden
  • Goede arbeidsvoorwaarden volgens cao en aanvullende regelingen


Enthousiast geworden?

descriptionHtml:
Deze vacature is onderdeel van onze werving & selectie dienstverlening en betreft een interne functie bij RGF Staffing.

Information Security Officer

Ben jij een ervaren informatiebeveiligingsspecialist met een scherp oog voor het zien van risico's op het gebied van IT Security en het nemen van de juiste acties om de risico’s te mitigeren? Heb je de senioriteit om organisatie breed IT Security beleid te implementeren en stakeholders mee te nemen in security-awareness en het volgen van jouw aanbevelingen? Dan is deze rol als Information Security Officer de baan voor jou!

Jouw rol

In deze functie ben je verantwoordelijk voor het borgen en doorontwikkelen van ons informatiebeveiligingsbeleid. Je hebt een proactieve én reactieve controlerende én adviserende rol en zorgt ervoor dat de organisatie voldoet aan zowel interne als externe security-eisen. Je werkt nauw samen met diverse stakeholders, van ontwikkelaars tot directie en je hebt de autoriteit om securitymaatregelen door te voeren. Daarnaast ben je verantwoordelijk voor het trainen van collega’s op het gebied van informatiebeveiliging en zorg te dragen voor awareness binnen hun eigen rol en verantwoordelijkheden.

Wat ga je doen?

Als Information Security Officer ben jij verantwoordelijk voor het bewaken en verder ontwikkelen van ons informatiebeveiligingsbeleid. Je zorgt ervoor dat het lokale beleid actueel blijft, in lijn is met de wereldwijde richtlijnen én aansluit bij de structuur van onze organisatie. Daarnaast draag je actief bij aan het vergroten van het bewustzijn rondom security binnen alle lagen van de organisatie.

Je werkzaamheden zijn breed en afwisselend. Zo houd jij je onder andere bezig met:

  • Het in kaart brengen van securitymaatregelen voor alle applicaties en systemen binnen de organisatie;
  • Het signaleren van beveiligingsrisico’s en gaps, en het proactief aandragen van oplossingen of mitigerende maatregelen;
  • Het proactief verzamelen, classificeren en prioriteren van IT-securityrisico’s in de bedrijfsvoering, en het bespreken hiervan met interne stakeholders en externe leveranciers;
  • Het beheren van IT-securitycontroles binnen het Risk Control Framework;
  • Het beoordelen van nieuwe leveranciers, producten, proposities en IT-wijzigingen op security-impact.

Daarnaast:

  • Creëer je awareness en draagvlak voor informatiebeveiliging door de hele organisatie;
  • Stel je businesscases en projectvoorstellen op voor securityverbeteringen en werk je aan het verkrijgen van draagvlak binnen de organisatie en de Board;
  • Bereid je security-audits voor en begeleid je deze;
  • Neem je de leiding bij security-incidenten en coördineer je de incident response processen.

Wat kan je van ons verwachten?

In deze functie krijg je een zelfstandige en strategische rol binnen de afdeling Legal, Risk & Compliance. Je opereert met een breed mandaat op het gebied van informatiebeveiliging en werkt nauw samen met de afdelingen IT Operations & Technology en Digital & Data. Je krijgt volop ruimte om je verder te ontwikkelen binnen het information security domein, bijvoorbeeld via opleidingen die je zelf mag kiezen. Daarnaast krijg je de kans om het securitybeleid van een dynamische en groeiende organisatie mee vorm te geven en daar daadwerkelijk de leiding in te nemen. Je werkt daarbij ook samen met een internationaal securityteam. Het hoofdkantoor bevindt zich in Almere en is op loopafstand van het Centraal Station.

Daarnaast bieden we uitstekende arbeidsvoorwaarden, zoals:

  • Een salaris tussen de €4.960 en €7.588 (op basis van 40 uur);
  • 8% vakantiegeld;
  • Een persoonlijk budget van 6% boven op je salaris en vakantiegeld. Dit kun je gebruiken voor extra vrije dagen, uitbetaling, een vitaliteitsregeling of fietsregeling;
  • Een goede pensioenregeling via a.s.r.;
  • Reiskostenvergoeding van €0,24 per kilometer of volledige OV-vergoeding;
  • Een internetvergoeding van €40 per maand (bij een fulltime dienstverband);
  • Hybride werken met flexibele kantoortijden;
  • Een laptop en telefoon van de zaak.

Wat verwachten wij van jou?

  • Je hebt sterke inhoudelijke kennis van informatiebeveiliging, zowel op beleidsmatig niveau als technisch;
  • Je beschikt over een scherp analytisch vermogen en een proactieve houding in het signaleren en mitigeren van risico’s;
  • Je communiceert uitstekend en weet je makkelijk te bewegen binnen alle lagen van de organisatie. Van directie en management tot ontwikkelaars en leveranciers;
  • Je neemt initiatief, bent besluitvaardig en weet snel de juiste stakeholders te betrekken bij besluitvorming;
  • Je bent nieuwsgierig naar de business en hebt de drive om continue te leren en de organisatie te versterken op het gebied van security-awareness;
  • Je beschikt over organisatie-inzicht en hebt een goed begrip van bedrijfsstructuren en processen.

Daarnaast breng je het volgende mee:

  • Minimaal 3 jaar ervaring in een vergelijkbare functie zoals Information Security Officer, Security Lead of Chief Information Security Officer;
  • Minimaal een WO werk- en denkniveau;
  • Je bent minimaal 32 tot 40 uur per week beschikbaar;
  • Aantoonbare kennis van en ervaring met relevantie certificeringen en security- en compliance-framework, zoals ISO 27001, NIST, CIS Controls, GDPR, Nis2 e.d.;
  • Ervaring met softwareontwikkeling is een pré.

Over RGF Staffing Nederland

Bij RGF Staffing Nederland draait werk om meer dan alleen een baan. Wij geloven in het creëren van kansen voor mensen, bedrijven en de samenleving. Als onderdeel van het internationale RGF Staffing, één van ’s werelds grootste HR-dienstverleners, zijn we actief in Europa, Azië, Australië en Noord-Amerika. Met ruim 1.400 collega’s in Nederland zetten we ons dagelijks in voor meer dan 47.000 uitzendkrachten en meer dan 2.100 klanten, vanuit meer dan 90 kantoren verspreid over het land.

Onze missie? Opportunities for Life. Faster, simpler & closer to you.

We verbinden mensen met werk en organisaties met talent, en helpen zo iedereen om zijn of haar passies na te jagen. Want hoe meer mensen doen wat ze écht raakt, hoe beter onze gezamenlijke toekomst. Bij RGF Staffing Nederland werken we met passie, dagen we de status quo uit en zetten we sociale meerwaarde altijd op één. Samen bouwen we aan een wereld waarin werk bijdraagt aan groei, welzijn en zingeving.

Volg je hart. Maak het verschil. Groei met ons mee.

Klaar om verschil te maken?

Wil jij impact maken binnen een organisatie waar jouw expertise echt telt? Solliciteer dan vandaag nog en neem de leiding in de verdere ontwikkeling van onze informatiebeveiliging! Klik op de knop om te solliciteren of neem contact op met Irene Jin, Head of Werving & Selectie via i.jin@ictrecht.nl of 06-34586890 voor meer informatie.

Note: Speaking and writing fluent Dutch is a requirement for this position.
