companyDescription:
Onze bioscopen zijn bijzonder geschikt voor zakelijke bijeenkomsten, digitale presentaties, conferenties, (film)evenementen en workshops. Vue Nederland organiseert regelmatig speciale acties en bijzondere avonden voor verschillende doelgroepen. Voor meer informatie over de mogelijkheden van Vue Zakelijk, kijk op: https://www.vuecinemas.nl/zakelijk

-Over Vue-
Vue Nederland is onderdeel van Vue International, de grootste bioscoopgroep buiten de Verenigde Staten en een van de belangrijkste spelers in de markt op het gebied van big screen entertainment.

Vue Nederland is gevestigd in Alkmaar, Alphen aan den Rijn, Amersfoort, Apeldoorn, Arnhem, Den Bosch, Deventer, Doetinchem, Eindhoven, Gorinchem, Heerhugowaard, Hilversum, Hoogeveen, Hoogezand, Hoorn, Kerkrade, Nijmegen, Purmerend en Vlaardingen.

Vue Nederland heeft twee Dolby Cinema's, gevestigd in onze bioscopen in Eindhoven en Hilversum. 
Dolby Cinema zorgt voor een complete ervaring in de bioscoop met intenste beelden, meeslepend geluid en een innovatief design. 

Het is de missie van Vue Nederland om de beste keuze in content te leveren gecombineerd met de beste technologie in de beste omgeving. Groei en innovatie zorgen voor een continue ontwikkeling en verbetering van de big screen entertainment ervaring. In een warme omgeving wordt iedereen een unieke beleving geboden; van een gastvrij ontvangst naar het beste in beeld, geluid en de meest comfortabele stoelen.

-Vue International-
Sinds zijn oprichting in 2003 in het Verenigd Koninkrijk heeft Vue zich internationaal gevestigd als exploitant en ontwikkelaar van moderne en innovatieve bioscopen.
Vanaf 2012 heeft Vue International verschillende Europese bioscoopketens verworven, waaronder Apollo Cinemas, CinemaxX, Multikino en Space Cinemas  en het Nederlandse JT Bioscopen in 2015, destijds het oudste bioscoopconcern van Nederland. Verdeeld over 10 landen heeft Vue 209 bioscopen, 1859 schermen en 8500 werknemers en is daarmee de grootste bioscoopketen in Europa.
descriptionHtml:
Vacature Assistent Manager bij Vue Amsterdam

Wil jij graag meehelpen om bioscoopbezoekers een fantastische filmervaring te geven? Stap in de rol van Assistent Manager! Zorg voor een uitstekende, gastvriendelijke dienstverlening én begeleid en coach je collega’s. Ga een nieuwe uitdaging aan in een unieke bioscoopsetting en geef je carrière een boost.


Assistent Manager Logistiek & Facilitair

Het beheren van de voorraad van Vue Cinemas: dát is jouw taak als Assistent Manager L&F. Je zoekt en selecteert reguliere leveranciers en plaatst bestellingen op basis van de voorraadadviezen. Bovendien ben je verantwoordelijk voor het optimaliseren en signaleren van voorraadniveaus en het controleren van facturen.


Met jouw proactieve instelling en sterke communicatieve vaardigheden weet je klachten of leveringsfouten met leveranciers razendsnel op te lossen. Verder ben je medeverantwoordelijk voor een efficiënte en optimale bedrijfsvoering in de bioscoop.


Jouw profiel

Voor de vacature Assistent Manager zijn wij op zoek naar een kandidaat die zich herkent in het volgende profiel:

  • Minimaal 21 jaar (vereiste voor de Horecavergunning);
  • Een werkweek van 39 uur per week;
  • Relevante opleiding afgerond (MBO- of HBO-diploma);
  • Een representatieve, flexibele teamplayer die ook zelfstandig kan werken;
  • Minimaal een jaar werkervaring in een leidinggevende functie;
  • In bezit van diploma Sociale Hygiëne en certificaat Bedrijfshulpverlening (BHV) (of bereid om deze te behalen);
  • Een gast- en servicegerichte instelling;
  • Uitstekende communicatieve en sociale vaardigheden;
  • Een proactieve en accurate instelling;
  • Commercieel vaardig;
  • Bereidheid tot avond -en weekendwerk;
  • Woonachtig in de regio.

  • Wij bieden jou

    Kom je ons leuke team versterken? Dan bieden wij jou:

    • Een unieke werkomgeving in een ontspannen sfeer;
    • Een afwisselende en uitdagende baan;
    • Een eerste overeenkomst die wordt aangegaan voor een periode van één jaar met, bij gebleken geschiktheid, uitzicht op verlenging;
    • Een salaris dat past bij jouw opleiding en ervaring;
    • Onbeperkt genieten van het filmaanbod van Vue Cinemas;
    • Korting op ons F&B-assortiment.

    Vacature Assistent Manager: solliciteer direct!

    Heb jij de ambitie om bezoekers, samen met een enthousiast en gemotiveerd team, de ultieme bioscoopervaring te geven? En ben jij de representatieve, flexibele teamplayer waar wij naar op zoek zijn? Solliciteer direct voor de vacature Assistent Manager. Wij komen graag met je in contact!


    Wil je nog wat meer informatie over Vue Cinemas? Bezoek www.vuecinemas.nl en ontdek alles over onze bioscopen.


    Over Vue Cinemas

    Bij Vue Cinemas zetten wij ons dag en nacht in om onze bezoekers een onvergetelijke filmbeleving te geven. Met onze gepassioneerde medewerkers, de beste content én de nieuwste filmtechnologie weten wij de verwachtingen van onze gasten keer op keer te overtreffen.

    Vue Cinemas is de grootste bioscoopketen in Europa. Met maar liefst 209 bioscopen, verdeeld over tien landen, zijn wij uitgegroeid tot één van de belangrijkste spelers op het gebied van big screen entertainment.

companyDescription: Keyrock is a global crypto investment firm offering industry-leading solutions in Market making, OTC, and Options for digital assets.
companySlogan: Changing the course of finance
descriptionHtml:
About Keyrock

Since our beginnings in 2017, we've grown to be a leading change-maker in the digital asset space, renowned for our partnerships and innovation.

Today, we rock with over 180 team members around the world. Our diverse team hails from 42 nationalities, with backgrounds ranging from DeFi natives to PhDs. Predominantly remote, we have hubs in London, Brussels, Singapore and Paris, and host regular online and offline hangouts to keep the crew tight.

We are trading on more than 80 exchanges, and working with a wide array of asset issuers. As a well-established market maker, our distinctive expertise led us to expand rapidly. Today, our services span market making, options trading, high-frequency trading, OTC, and DeFi trading desks.

But we’re more than a service provider. We’re an initiator. We're pioneers in adopting the Rust Development language for our algorithmic trading, and champions of its use in the industry. We support the growth of Web3 startups through our Accelerator Program. We upgrade ecosystems by injecting liquidity into promising DeFi, RWA, and NFT protocols. And we push the industry's progress with our research and governance initiatives.

At Keyrock, we're not just envisioning the future of digital assets. We're actively building it.

Mission statement

We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled Automation Engineer to lead the development and maintenance of automation systems that validate our critical business processes and drive measurable quality across the organisation. The ideal candidate will play a key role in building automated test coverage, performance monitoring, and deployment validation pipelines, while also ensuring the broader QA framework is effectively adopted across teams. The candidate will be focused on building out these automated testing frameworks for cryptocurrency market making technology, with inherent high availability and quality standards. Familiarity with similar systems will be helpful.

This role is highly cross-functional: you will collaborate with product, engineering, and delivery to implement best practices that support scalable, reliable systems.

Job Description

Key Responsibilities:

You will report to a Delivery Manager, and be guided by a QA Capability Lead to align practices across the business:

  • Design, implement, and maintain automation frameworks for validating end-to-end business processes and critical system functions.
  • Develop test strategies that support key QA framework KPIs, including coverage of critical business logic, process adherence, change failure rates, and performance monitoring.
  • Build and integrate automated testing into CI/CD pipelines to enable fast, safe releases.
  • Partner with Product and Delivery Managers to drive adoption of standard QA processes across teams.
  • Support the creation and maintenance of high-quality documentation for critical business functions and features.
  • Implement monitoring and alerting to ensure production stability and fast incident response.
  • Analyse trends in deployment success/failures and performance degradations to proactively improve system quality.
  • Track and report progress on quality metrics and KPIs to stakeholders.
  • Contribute to operational runbooks for incident response and system recovery.
  • Mentor engineers and QA team members on automation and testing best practices.

Background And Experience

  • Proven experience designing and maintaining automation frameworks (backend and functional).
  • Proficiency with modern automation tools (e.g., Playwright, Cypress, Python, JavaScript/TypeScript).
  • Strong understanding of QA frameworks and standard processes (process adherence, test coverage, KPI-driven quality tracking).
  • Experience with CI/CD pipelines and automated quality gates.
  • Understanding & monitoring of unit testing coverage.
  • Familiarity with API testing and backend validation (REST, gRPC).
  • Strong ability to link tests to business-critical functionality and risk-based coverage.
  • Solid knowledge of performance monitoring and alerting tools (e.g., Datadog, Grafana) and production health monitoring.
  • Strong experience with version control (Git) and issue tracking (Jira, Shortcut).
  • Incident management and root cause analysis (Change Failure Rate, MTTR).
  • Excellent collaboration and communication skills with cross-functional teams.
  • Ability to drive documentation and traceability efforts for critical functionality.
  • Familiarity with test management tools (Testmo, TestRail) and QA reporting.
  • Business analysis skills to support identifying critical business logic.
  • Self-starter with excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
  • Familiarity with UI testing and web browser automation
  • An interest in the cryptocurrency industry and market making is a plus.

Our recruitment philosophy

We value self-awareness and powerful communication skills in our recruitment process. We seek fiercely passionate people who understand themselves and their career goals. We're after those with the right skills and a conscious choice to join our field. The perfect fit? A crypto enthusiast who’s driven, collaborative, acts with ownership and delivers solid, scalable outcomes.

Our offer

  • A competitive salary package
  • Autonomy in your time management thanks to flexible working hours and the opportunity to work remotely
  • The freedom to create your own entrepreneurial experience by being part of a team of people in search of excellence

As an employer we are committed to building a positive and collaborative work environment. We welcome employees of all backgrounds, and hire, reward and promote entirely based on merit and performance.

Due to the nature of our business and external requirements, we perform background checks on all potential employees, passing which is a prerequisite to join Keyrock.

We're pioneers in adopting the Rust Development language for our algorithmic trading, and champions of its use in the industry. We support the growth of Web3 startups through our Accelerator Program. We upgrade ecosystems by injecting liquidity into promising DeFi, RWA, and NFT protocols. And we push the industry's progress with our research and governance initiatives.At Keyrock, we're not just envisioning the future of digital assets. We're actively building it.Mission statementWe are seeking a highly motivated and skilled Automation Engineer to lead the development and maintenance of automation systems that validate our critical business processes and drive measurable quality across the organisation. The ideal candidate will play a key role in building automated test coverage, performance monitoring, and deployment validation pipelines, while also ensuring the broader QA framework is effectively adopted across teams. The candidate will be focused on building out these automated testing frameworks for cryptocurrency market making technology, with inherent high availability and quality standards. Familiarity with similar systems will be helpful.This role is highly cross-functional: you will collaborate with product, engineering, and delivery to implement best practices that support scalable, reliable systems.Job DescriptionKey Responsibilities:You will report to a Delivery Manager, and be guided by a QA Capability Lead to align practices across the business:Design, implement, and maintain automation frameworks for validating end-to-end business processes and critical system functions. Develop test strategies that support key QA framework KPIs, including coverage of critical business logic, process adherence, change failure rates, and performance monitoring. Build and integrate automated testing into CI/CD pipelines to enable fast, safe releases. Partner with Product and Delivery Managers to drive adoption of standard QA processes across teams. Support the creation and maintenance of high-quality documentation for critical business functions and features. Implement monitoring and alerting to ensure production stability and fast incident response. Analyse trends in deployment success/failures and performance degradations to proactively improve system quality. Track and report progress on quality metrics and KPIs to stakeholders. Contribute to operational runbooks for incident response and system recovery. Mentor engineers and QA team members on automation and testing best practices. Background And ExperienceProven experience designing and maintaining automation frameworks (backend and functional). Proficiency with modern automation tools (e.g., Playwright, Cypress, Python, JavaScript/TypeScript). Strong understanding of QA frameworks and standard processes (process adherence, test coverage, KPI-driven quality tracking). Experience with CI/CD pipelines and automated quality gates. Understanding & monitoring of unit testing coverage. Familiarity with API testing and backend validation (REST, gRPC). Strong ability to link tests to business-critical functionality and risk-based coverage. Solid knowledge of performance monitoring and alerting tools (e.g., Datadog, Grafana) and production health monitoring. Strong experience with version control (Git) and issue tracking (Jira, Shortcut). Incident management and root cause analysis (Change Failure Rate, MTTR). Excellent collaboration and communication skills with cross-functional teams. Ability to drive documentation and traceability efforts for critical functionality. Familiarity with test management tools (Testmo, TestRail) and QA reporting. Business analysis skills to support identifying critical business logic. Self-starter with excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail. Familiarity with UI testing and web browser automationAn interest in the cryptocurrency industry and market making is a plus. Our recruitment philosophyWe value self-awareness and powerful communication skills in our recruitment process. We seek fiercely passionate people who understand themselves and their career goals. We're after those with the right skills and a conscious choice to join our field. The perfect fit? A crypto enthusiast who’s driven, collaborative, acts with ownership and delivers solid, scalable outcomes.Our offerA competitive salary packageAutonomy in your time management thanks to flexible working hours and the opportunity to work remotelyThe freedom to create your own entrepreneurial experience by being part of a team of people in search of excellenceAs an employer we are committed to building a positive and collaborative work environment. We welcome employees of all backgrounds, and hire, reward and promote entirely based on merit and performance.Due to the nature of our business and external requirements, we perform background checks on all potential employees, passing which is a prerequisite to join Keyrock.https://keyrock.com/careers/

descriptionHtml:
职位来源于智联招聘。

两个岗位方向：

一、资深外联

  • 作为公司在刚果金地区的主要对外联络人，利用流利的法语与当地政府部门、监管机构、行业协会等建立、拓展并维护良好且稳固的合作关系。
  • 针对公司在刚果金口岸项目，负责与当地政府、供应商、客户等进行法语沟通协调，确保项目各个环节顺利进行。
  • 运用出色的法语沟通能力和人际交往技巧，收集刚果金市场的商业信息、竞争对手情报等，为公司的战略决策提供有力支持。重点分析当地市场趋势、潜在机会和风险，协助公司制定相应的市场策略和应对措施。
  • 在遇到涉及公司利益的突发事件或危机情况时，迅速启动应急响应机制，运用法语与当地各方沟通协调，积极化解矛盾，维护公司的良好形象和合法权益。

二、人事行政

  • 口岸基地的人事行政工作；
  • 内部内务活动；
  • 中方外派员工人事管理以及对接本地三方人力；

要求：法语专业本科及以上学历优先，人事岗位1年以上相关工作经验/外联岗位5年以上外联外事经验，优秀的笔译和口译能力，了解文化差异和语言习惯，良好的沟通能力和团队合作精神。

外联薪资开放，可面议。

以担保或任何理由索要财物，扣押证照，均涉嫌违法。

descriptionHtml:
Locatie Amsterdam

Salaris € 3.471,- tot € 6.114,-

Dienstverband Parttime of fulltime, 16-40 uur, 0,4-1,0 fte

Contract Tijdelijk

Wij zoeken één of meerdere professionals die onze gemotiveerde studenten kennis laten maken met het brede vakgebied van de cybersecurity.

Want waar anders dan bij de HvA …

  • Help je de wereld veiliger maken doordat je nieuwe ICT-ers en information security specialisten opleidt;
  • Kun je je kennis over information security delen;
  • Blijf je op de hoogte van de nieuwste tools en technieken;
  • Werk je samen met toonaangevende securitybedrijven en organisaties;

Dit zegt jouw toekomstige collega

De lesstof die ik nu geef, kan volgend jaar verouderd zijn. Continu zijn we bezig met nadenken over hetgeen nu het meest relevant is om aan de student mee te geven. Daarbij zie je studenten binnen komen die in korte tijd zich enorm ontwikkelen tot startende professional. Dat is belonend werk. Tim Langstraat, docent Cybersecurity

Wil je ontdekken of deze baan iets voor jou is? Bel of app Tiemen Mink, teamcoordinator Cybersecurity (06 22511471) of mail naar t.mink@hva.nl voor een eerste kennismaking.

Zo maak jij het verschil

Als docent verzorg je onderwijs over bijvoorbeeld incident response, monitoring, ethical hacking, Malware, Cryptografie, Threat Intelligence en pentesten. Maar ook over basiselementen van cybersecurity, zoals het OSI of het TCP/IP model bij netwerken, besturingssystemen zoals Linux en Windows en scripten in bash en python. Daarnaast begeleid je studenten bij hun projectwerk en bij stage en afstuderen. Je weet veel van de inhoud, maar je hebt ook ideeën hoe je de lesstof tot leven brengt.

Bij HBO-ICT wordt de combinatie van (technische) kennis en professionele vaardigheden gecombineerd en toegepast in situaties in de (gesimuleerde) praktijk. Studenten leren in twee (AD) of vier (Bachelor) jaar problemen te herkennen en op te lossen. Jouw coachende rol inspireert hen daarin het beste uit zichzelf te halen.

Zo verrijk jij ons

Op de HvA zijn geen twee medewerkers hetzelfde. Dus wie je ook bent, je vult ons team altijd aan. We zoeken bevlogen professionals die de vaardigheid hebben om zich nieuwe tools en technieken eigen te maken. Je hebt een resultaatgerichte teammentaliteit, neemt graag initiatief en verantwoordelijkheid. Je bent communicatief sterk en kunt op een coachende wijze de studenten zowel de techniek leren als hen dit professioneel toe te laten passen.

Verder neem je mee:

  • Een afgeronde relevante hbo- of academische opleiding bij voorkeur op masterniveau
  • Kennis en relevante ervaring op het gebied van cybersecurity;
  • Een didactische bevoegdheid, of de bereidheid deze binnen een vastgestelde periode te behalen. Deze opleiding biedt de HvA je aan.

Nog geen ervaring in het onderwijs? Geen punt!

Je krijgt een buddy en een inwerktraject (0,5 - 1,5 jaar) dat volledig op jou is toegespitst. Ook loop je eerst mee met ervaren collega’s. Daarnaast krijg je de mogelijkheid om je didactische bevoegdheid te halen. En wist je dat onze docenten een deel van hun werktijd en een mooi budget mogen inzetten voor hun vakinhoudelijke en persoonlijke ontwikkeling?

Wat je verder krijgt

  • De keuze of je fulltime, parttime of in de avonduren wilt werken. Ideaal als je deze functie wilt combineren met bijvoorbeeld een baan in het bedrijfsleven.
  • Een bruto maandsalaris tussen € 3.471,- tot € 6.114,- (schaal 10/11 cao hbo).
  • Een eindejaarsuitkering van 8,3% en vakantiegeld van 8%van je bruto jaarsalaris.
  • De mogelijkheid om het vakantieverlof uit te breiden naar 428 vakantie-uren per jaar
  • Recht op verschillende soorten verlof, zoals (on)betaald ouderschapsverlof, zorgverlof, adoptie- en pleegzorgverlof en regelingen voor wat oudere medewerkers.
  • Afhankelijk van je situatie, kun je gebruikmaken van een van de volgende mogelijkheden: kilometervergoeding voor je woon-werkverkeer, een NS-Business Card, een eigen HvA-fiets of belastingvoordeel bij de aanschaf van een andere fiets. Kun je, bijvoorbeeld vanwege een functiebeperking, niet met eigen of openbaar vervoer komen? Dan kijken we samen naar de mogelijkheden.
  • Korting op onder meer boeken, culturele cursussen, soft- en hardware en een aantal verzekeringen.
  • Volop aandacht voor werkplezier en werk-privébalans. En voor vitaliteit. Zo kun je voordelig deelnemen aan onze sportactiviteiten speciaal voor HvA-medewerkers of via sportcentrum USC. Ook kun je gebruikmaken van onze (loopbaan)coaches en van online tools die je ondersteunen bij je mentale gezondheid.
  • Een goede pensioenregeling bij ABP.
  • Een maandelijkse internetvergoeding en een laptop en telefoon in bruikleen.
  • Last but not least: een werkplek in een dynamische omgeving waarin je in een fijne sfeer met collega’s werkt, die je ook buiten het werk om kunt ontmoeten, bijvoorbeeld via onze community’s, zoals HvAPride, HvA Alumni, HvA United, JongHvA en FEM IT.

Bekijk hier al onze arbeidsvoorwaarden en mogelijkheden op maat.

Word onze nieuwe Docent Cybersecurity

  • Upload je motivatie en cv via de sollicitatiebutton. En houd je inbox in de gaten: binnen twee tot drie weken ontvang je een persoonlijke reactie.
  • Wil je eerst meer weten over de functie? Tiemen Mink kan je er alles over vertellen. Bel of app via 06-22511471 of mail naar t.mink@hva.nl.
  • Voor meer informatie over de sollicitatieprocedure kun je mailen naar vacatures@hva.nl.

Creating tomorrow op de Faculteit FDMCI/ Opleiding HBO-ICT

De opleiding HBO-ICT bestaat uit een veelzijdig en enthousiast docententeam van 200 collega’s die samen ruim 3000 studenten opleiden. De opleiding behoort tot de faculteit Digitale Media en Creatieve Industrie en is gevestigd op de Amstelcampus in hartje Amsterdam. Binnen onze grote opleiding ga je onderdeel uitmaken van een klein, divers en gezellig team, die kwaliteit hoog in het vaandel hebben staan. Meer informatie over de opleiding HBO-ICT vind je op www.hva.nl/hbo-ict.

companyDescription: IVO Rechtspraak ontwikkelt en beheert digitale systemen voor de rechtspraak. Toch gaat het bij ons niet om nullen en enen, maar om mensen: burgers, rechters, het Openbaar Ministerie en advocaten. Maar ook partijen als de politie, slachtofferhulp of de kinderbescherming. Jouw oplossingen maken dat de rechtspraak in Nederland beter en sneller werkt, begrijpelijk en toegankelijk is. Jij draagt dus bij aan een rechtvaardige samenleving.
descriptionHtml:
Ontwerp mee aan het IT-landschap van de Rechtspraak. En draag bij aan een digitale rechtspraak die voor iedereen werkt!

Jij ontwerpt mee aan het IT-landschap van de Rechtspraak. Niet zomaar een baan dus! Jij vertaalt de vragen van onze klanten naar veilige, betrouwbare en doelmatige oplossingen. Die klanten? Dat zijn je meer dan 12.000 collega’s bij de Rechtspraak. Van rechters, griffiers en bodes tot juridisch en administratief medewerkers. Zo draag jij bij aan een digitale rechtspraak die voor iedereen werkt!

Dit doe je bij IVO Rechtspraak

Jij bent de bruggenbouwer tussen strategie en uitvoering in onze digitale transformatie. Als Solution Architect IT Service Management & Control vertaal je onze nieuwe doelarchitectuur naar concrete processen en richtlijnen. Je helpt de Rechtspraak bij het vormgeven van een modern, wendbaar en veilig IT-servicemanagement landschap. Een essentiële rol in één van de belangrijkste digitaliseringsprojecten binnen de Nederlandse overheid!

Als Solution Architect ITSM & Control sta je aan de wieg van een nieuwe manier van werken binnen de Rechtspraak. Jij overziet hoe we afscheid nemen van traditioneel beheer en ontwikkeling, en begeleidt de transitie naar een modern servicemanagement model. Je ontwerpt de processen, richtlijnen en kaders die nodig zijn om onze IT-dienstverlening betrouwbaar, schaalbaar en wendbaar te maken.

Je werkt samen met value teams binnen jou base en erbuiten om het ITSM-landschap te herzien. Waar moeten we naar Service Integration & Management? Welke monitoring-tooling past bij onze doelarchitectuur en ondersteunt tevens de rijkstandaarden BIO op risk en compliance? Hoe richten we change- en releasemanagement in binnen onze DevOps-werkwijze? En hoe zorgen we dat securitybeheer, incident response en compliance naadloos geïntegreerd worden in onze nieuwe werkwijze? Daarnaast is kennis van moderne ontwikkeling zoals CI/CD en automation van processen een vereiste om deze transitie succesvol te maken.

Jij vormt het bruggenhoofd tussen architectuur en operatie. Je vertaalt de principes uit de doelarchitectuur naar concrete richtlijnen voor onze base teams. Je werkt nauw samen met domeinarchitecten, product owners en DevOps-teams om ervoor te zorgen dat het nieuwe ITSM-framework zowel theoretisch klopt als praktisch uitvoerbaar is. Je houdt daarbij de balans tussen stabiliteit en innovatie, tussen compliance en wendbaarheid. Tevens moeten de huidige IVO bedrijfsprocessen aangepast worden naar een moderne inrichting die deze nieuwe werkwijze optimaal ondersteunt.

Daarnaast ben je medeverantwoordelijk voor het opzetten van een modern Control Framework. Dit framework moet in lijn zijn met de NIS2 en BIO normen van de rijksoverheid en worden vertaald naar duidelijke maatregelen die de organisatie kan gebruiken. Je definieert hoe we grip houden op de kwaliteit, veiligheid en prestaties van onze dienstverlening. Je zorgt dat we niet alleen agile kunnen ontwikkelen, maar ook betrouwbaar kunnen opereren binnen de hoge eisen die aan de Rechtspraak worden gesteld.

Ben je een visionaire architect die zowel thuis is in moderne IT4IT-frameworks als in de menselijke kant van digitale transformatie? Vind je het een uitdaging om complexe veranderingen te structureren en teams hierin te begeleiden? Dan is deze rol bij IVO Rechtspraak op je lijf geschreven!

Collega aan het woord

‘Het allerleukste aan m’n werk? Dat ik met zoveel onderwerpen bezig ben, op zoveel gebieden. Dat gaat van een elektronische handtekening tot applicaties voor HRM of finance. Het is niet alleen oplossingen uitdenken, maar ook zorgen dat ze binnen de bestaande IT-omgeving passen. Zien dat een idee goed werkt in de uitvoering, dat geeft echt een kick! De Rechtspraak moet onafhankelijk zijn, dat speelt ook een rol bij de keuzes die gemaakt worden bij het ontwerpen. De context is toch anders dan in het bedrijfsleven. Voor mij is het belangrijk om werk van betekenis te doen.’

Ruud, solution architect

Pluspunten van deze functie

Om te beginnen een leuke, informele werksfeer. We houden de lijnen graag kort! En je arbeidsvoorwaarden zijn prima geregeld volgens de CAO Rijk. Wat belangrijke punten op een rij:

  • Contract: een jaarcontract voor 32 tot 36 uur per week, met uitzicht op een vast dienstverband.
  • Salaris: je komt terecht in salarisschaal 12 (maximaal € 6.907,67 per maand bij een werkweek van 36 uur). Inschaling vindt plaats op basis van je werkervaring.
  • Keuzebudget: 16,5% van je jaarsalaris (maximaal € 1.139) om je eigen arbeidsvoorwaarden samen te stellen. Zoals het kopen van verlof, het aanschaffen van een fiets of het verduurzamen van je woning.
  • Doorgroeimogelijkheden: je functie zit vol mogelijkheden om je kennis verder uit te breiden en je persoonlijke ontwikkeling te stimuleren. En bij onze IVO Academie kun je allerlei trainingen en opleidingen doen.
  • Werk-privébalans: je bent vrij om je werkdagen flexibel in te delen. Zo kun je deels vanuit huis werken. We dragen € 750 bij aan het inrichten van een comfortabele thuiswerkplek.
  • Vakanties en vrije dagen: je hebt 144 vakantie- en 64 keuzebudget-uren op basis van een 36-urige werkweek. Dat zijn dus in totaal 26 dagen.
  • Gratis OV: je krijgt van ons een OV-chipkaart voor je woon-werkverkeer.
  • Pensioen: je bouwt je pensioen op bij het ABP, een van de grootste pensioenfondsen van Nederland. Ook dat is dus goed geregeld.
  • Leuke activiteiten: een teamdag, borrelmiddagen, een after summer-feest … er gebeurt van alles. Of organiseer ook zelf iets met je collega’s, van padel tot yoga of een pubquiz.

Meer informatie over onze arbeidsvoorwaarden vind je op www.p-direkt.nl.

Wat we van jou verwachten

Dit zijn de dingen waar je volgens het ideaalplaatje aan voldoet:

  • Je denkt en werkt minimaal op hbo-niveau
  • Je hebt minimaal 5 jaar ervaring als Solution Architect
  • Diepgaande kennis van en praktische ervaring met moderne ITSM-frameworks zoals ITIL v4
  • Ervaring met het ontwerpen en implementeren van ITSM-processen en dit ook specifiek binnen een DevOps-context
  • Je bent gecertificeerd voor TOGAF en ArchiMate
  • Je bent vertrouwd met traditionele en Cloud gebaseerde omgevingen en/of containerarchitecturen
  • Je weet hoe het is om in een agile omgeving te werken

Kom je bij ons werken, dan vragen we ook altijd een Verklaring Omtrent Gedrag.

Waar kom je te werken?

Bij IVO Rechtspraak werken rond de 1.000 bevlogen, trotse en betrokken professionals. Samen ontwikkelen en beheren we de digitale systemen voor de rechtspraak. Zo zorgen we dat rechtszaken iedere dag soepel verlopen. Onze kernwaarde integriteit is verweven in het DNA van al onze medewerkers. Van IT’er tot communicatieadviseur of beleidsmedewerker. Daarom vinden wij het ook belangrijk dat iedereen die bij ons werkt zich thuis voelt, plezier heeft in het werk en zichzelf kan zijn in een informele werksfeer.

IVO Rechtspraak >

Meer informatie over de functie of het sollicitatieproces?

Is een baan als Solution Architect IT Service Management & Control bij IVO Rechtspraak iets voor jou? Reageer dan via onderstaande button. Reageer snel, omdat we mogelijk al eerder met interessante kandidaten in gesprek gaan. Het kan zijn dat de vacature eerder sluit.

Meer weten? Bel Edwin Visser, Corporate Recruiter, via 06 554 81 826.

Digitaal koffiedrinken?

Benieuwd hoe het is om bij IVO Rechtspraak te werken? Plan een digitaal kopje koffie in met een van onze collega's.

Koffie met IVO >

Solliciteren bij IVO Rechtspraak

De sollicitatieprocedure bij IVO Rechtspraak loopt via onderstaande stappen. Hiermee willen we zo veel mogelijk over jou te weten komen, maar laten we je ook kennismaken met ons.

Sollicitatie en selectie

Zodra je hebt gesolliciteerd, beoordelen we je cv en motivatiebrief op de genoemde functie-eisen.

Oriënterende kennismaking (online)

Sluit je profiel voldoende aan? Dan maken we graag oriënterend kennis met je.

Vervolggesprek (op locatie)

Als er bij het eerste gesprek van beide kanten een match is, plannen we een tweede gesprek. Daarbij ontmoet je ook collega's uit je mogelijk toekomstige team.

descriptionText: Ontwerp mee aan het IT-landschap van de Rechtspraak. En draag bij aan een digitale rechtspraak die voor iedereen werkt!Jij ontwerpt mee aan het IT-landschap van de Rechtspraak. Niet zomaar een baan dus! Jij vertaalt de vragen van onze klanten naar veilige, betrouwbare en doelmatige oplossingen. Die klanten? Dat zijn je meer dan 12.000 collega’s bij de Rechtspraak. Van rechters, griffiers en bodes tot juridisch en administratief medewerkers. Zo draag jij bij aan een digitale rechtspraak die voor iedereen werkt!Dit doe je bij IVO RechtspraakJij bent de bruggenbouwer tussen strategie en uitvoering in onze digitale transformatie. Als Solution Architect IT Service Management & Control vertaal je onze nieuwe doelarchitectuur naar concrete processen en richtlijnen. Je helpt de Rechtspraak bij het vormgeven van een modern, wendbaar en veilig IT-servicemanagement landschap. Een essentiële rol in één van de belangrijkste digitaliseringsprojecten binnen de Nederlandse overheid!Als Solution Architect ITSM & Control sta je aan de wieg van een nieuwe manier van werken binnen de Rechtspraak. Jij overziet hoe we afscheid nemen van traditioneel beheer en ontwikkeling, en begeleidt de transitie naar een modern servicemanagement model. Je ontwerpt de processen, richtlijnen en kaders die nodig zijn om onze IT-dienstverlening betrouwbaar, schaalbaar en wendbaar te maken.Je werkt samen met value teams binnen jou base en erbuiten om het ITSM-landschap te herzien. Waar moeten we naar Service Integration & Management? Welke monitoring-tooling past bij onze doelarchitectuur en ondersteunt tevens de rijkstandaarden BIO op risk en compliance? Hoe richten we change- en releasemanagement in binnen onze DevOps-werkwijze? En hoe zorgen we dat securitybeheer, incident response en compliance naadloos geïntegreerd worden in onze nieuwe werkwijze? Daarnaast is kennis van moderne ontwikkeling zoals CI/CD en automation van processen een vereiste om deze transitie succesvol te maken.Jij vormt het bruggenhoofd tussen architectuur en operatie. Je vertaalt de principes uit de doelarchitectuur naar concrete richtlijnen voor onze base teams. Je werkt nauw samen met domeinarchitecten, product owners en DevOps-teams om ervoor te zorgen dat het nieuwe ITSM-framework zowel theoretisch klopt als praktisch uitvoerbaar is. Je houdt daarbij de balans tussen stabiliteit en innovatie, tussen compliance en wendbaarheid. Tevens moeten de huidige IVO bedrijfsprocessen aangepast worden naar een moderne inrichting die deze nieuwe werkwijze optimaal ondersteunt.Daarnaast ben je medeverantwoordelijk voor het opzetten van een modern Control Framework. Dit framework moet in lijn zijn met de NIS2 en BIO normen van de rijksoverheid en worden vertaald naar duidelijke maatregelen die de organisatie kan gebruiken. Je definieert hoe we grip houden op de kwaliteit, veiligheid en prestaties van onze dienstverlening. Je zorgt dat we niet alleen agile kunnen ontwikkelen, maar ook betrouwbaar kunnen opereren binnen de hoge eisen die aan de Rechtspraak worden gesteld.Ben je een visionaire architect die zowel thuis is in moderne IT4IT-frameworks als in de menselijke kant van digitale transformatie? Vind je het een uitdaging om complexe veranderingen te structureren en teams hierin te begeleiden? Dan is deze rol bij IVO Rechtspraak op je lijf geschreven!Collega aan het woord‘Het allerleukste aan m’n werk? Dat ik met zoveel onderwerpen bezig ben, op zoveel gebieden. Dat gaat van een elektronische handtekening tot applicaties voor HRM of finance. Het is niet alleen oplossingen uitdenken, maar ook zorgen dat ze binnen de bestaande IT-omgeving passen. Zien dat een idee goed werkt in de uitvoering, dat geeft echt een kick! De Rechtspraak moet onafhankelijk zijn, dat speelt ook een rol bij de keuzes die gemaakt worden bij het ontwerpen. De context is toch anders dan in het bedrijfsleven. Voor mij is het belangrijk om werk van betekenis te doen.’Ruud, solution architectPluspunten van deze functieOm te beginnen een leuke, informele werksfeer. We houden de lijnen graag kort! companyDescription:
Sinclair offers a transformative, lifelong partnership through our powerhouse of proven products that work harmoniously to rejuvenate, stimulate and enhance. We are architects of change, visionaries of self-expression, and pioneers of holistic aesthetics. With expertise, innovation, and a commitment to progress, we empower clinicians with the tools and knowledge to elevate their practice and redefine possibilities.

Founded in 1971, Sinclair is a global medical aesthetics company, that delivers an extensive product range. With an in-house commercial infrastructure, including manufacturing, company-owned affiliates and a network of distributors in leading global markets. Our products are sold in 55 countries worldwide. Sinclair was acquired by Huadong Medicine Ltd in 2018.
companySlogan: A global aesthetics company. Headquartered in London and a subsidiary of Huadong Medicine Ltd.
descriptionHtml:
Description Essential Required Skills And Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Facility Management, Environmental Health and Safety, or a related field.

Relevant certifications in health and safety (e.g., OSHA, VCA) and emergency response (BHV) preferred.

Minimum of 3-5 years of experience in facility management, health and safety, or a related role.

Valid Dutch Prevention Officer certification (Prevention Officer) is a plus.

Fluent in Dutch and English (written and spoken).

Desired Required Skills And Qualifications

In-depth knowledge of environmental, health, and safety regulations, particularly in the Netherlands.

Working in pharma or food environment

Selfstarter

Strong organizational and teme management skills

Effective leadership and communication abilities to manage a team of BHV responders and interact with employees at all levels.

Ability to assess risks and develop preventive measures.

Proficient in using facility management software and systems.

Strong problem-solving and decision-making capabilities, particularly in emergencies.

Familiarity with Dutch workplace health and safety laws and regulations.

Crisis management skills and the ability to stay calm under pressure.

Key Responsibilities 1. Facility Management Activities:

Oversee general facility operations, including maintenance, repairs, and general upkeep of buildings, equipment, and grounds.

Ensure that the facility is compliant with legal regulations related to environmental sustainability and workplace safety.

Manage facility-related vendors and service contracts (e.g., cleaning, security, HVAC, etc.).

Develop and manage the facility's annual budget and ensure cost-effective practices.

Ensure proper maintenance of facility systems, including lighting, HVAC, and waste management.

  • Environment, Health, and Safety (EH&S):

Implement and manage EH&S policies and programs to ensure a safe and healthy working environment for all employees.

Conduct regular safety audits and inspections to identify potential hazards and ensure compliance with local and international safety regulations.

Report on safety incidents and accidents, ensuring timely resolution and compliance with incident reporting standards.

Organize and lead safety training programs for employees, ensuring everyone is aware of safety procedures and emergency protocols.

Continuously evaluate and improve the workplace's safety performance.

  • BHV (Emergency Response Team):

Serve as the Head of the BHV (Bedrijfshulp verlener) emergency response team.

Coordinate emergency response plans and drills (including fire drills, evacuation procedures, and first aid).

Maintain and update emergency response documentation, ensuring the facility is well-prepared for emergencies.

Provide guidance and support to BHV members to ensure they are adequately trained and equipped.

  • Prevention Officer Responsibilities:

Act as the designated Prevention Officer, identifying potential workplace hazards and taking preventive measures to minimize risks.

Maintain and implement health and safety policies in line with Dutch regulations and company standards.

Conduct regular safety meetings and communicate with management regarding any risks or safety concerns.

Work closely with external safety authorities to ensure full compliance with Dutch safety laws.

Benefits

Currency

Duration

Salary Maximum 0.00

Salary Minimum 0.00

Location Almere

Job Details

A new role for our location reporting into our Plant Manager:

The Facility Officer is responsible for the Environment, Health, and Safety (EH&S) on site, managing the company’s Emergency Response (BHV) team, ensuring the role of Prevention Officer is maintained, and overseeing day-to-day facility management operations.

This role is essential to maintaining a safe, efficient, and compliant work environment.

Job Competencies

  • Leadership: Ability to lead teams, particularly the BHV emergency response team, with a calm and authoritative presence.
  • Communication: Strong interpersonal skills to communicate with employees, management, and external vendors.
  • Detail-Oriented: A keen eye for detail to ensure compliance with safety regulations and standards.
  • Problem Solving: Able to identify and resolve issues quickly, especially during emergencies.
  • Technical Acumen: Understanding of facility systems and their maintenance requirements.
  • Time Management: Ability to manage multiple tasks efficiently and prioritize responsibilities.

Benefits

Currency

Duration

Salary Maximum 0.00

Salary Minimum 0.00

companyDescription:
Atolls is Europe’s largest shopping engagement platform with an international footprint in more than 20 markets.

We provide people with access to the best savings, cashback, deals, product inspiration, and reviews. Our shopping destinations help millions of consumers spend online with confidence.
companySlogan: The World's Leading Destinations for Decisions.
descriptionHtml:
Atolls is the world’s largest community-driven shopping platform, active in over 20 markets worldwide.

After rebranding from Global Savings Group to Atolls, we’ve grown from a coupon provider into a global powerhouse, empowering consumers at every step of their shopping journeys. As a trusted destination, we help millions make smarter, more rewarding spending decisions through savings, cashback, deals, product inspiration, and reviews. Our mission is to support consumers from Anywhere to Purchase (A to P), making shopping simpler, smarter, and more enjoyable.

Our team of over 1,000 colleagues, representing 70 nationalities across 10 countries, works together to build digital products that transform the shopping experience. We foster a culture of innovation, learning, and collaboration, giving you the opportunity to grow, lead, and make meaningful contributions. Whether you’re shaping products or improving user experiences, you’ll be empowered to leave your mark and drive real impact.

Joining Atolls means becoming part of a global team that is constantly evolving and creating platforms relied on by millions of users for confident, seamless shopping experiences.

About This Role:

You’ll be our Workday HRIS Integration Specialist, building, running, and improving integrations across Workday and the rest of our stack. You’ll work with HRIS product manager, People & Culture, Payroll, Finance, and IT to ship reliable feeds, APIs, and events, and you’ll own the lifecycle from design to support. Expect hands‑on work with Workday EIB, Studio, Core/Cloud Connectors, PECI, SOAP/REST APIs, and RaaS.

Responsibilities:

In this role, you will:

  • Design, build, test, and deploy Workday integrations (inbound/outbound) using EIB, Studio, and Core/Cloud Connectors.
  • Develop/ maintain and support payroll interfaces.
  • Build API integrations using Workday SOAP and REST services; implement OAuth 2.0 for REST and credentials for SOAP as appropriate.
  • Deliver RaaS (Reports‑as‑a‑Service) integrations for bulk/reporting data needs.
  • Set up secure transports (SFTP/HTTPS), PGP encryption, and certificate management.
  • Model and map data for HCM, Absence, Compensation & Benefits, and Finance objects; handle deltas and effectively manage data.
  • Define and manage Workday Integration System Users (ISU) and Integration System Security Groups (ISSG) with least‑privilege domain access.
  • Write functional and technical specs; create mapping docs and sequence diagrams.
  • Plan and run unit, SIT, and UAT; promote to prod using change control.
  • Monitor integrations, build dashboards/alerts, and handle incident response with root‑cause fixes.
  • Partner with the Product Manager on requirements and roadmap.


Your Profile:

  • 4+ years in Workday integrations, with production ownership.
  • Strong with EIB, Studio, and Core/Cloud Connectors.
  • Hands‑on with payroll interfaces.
  • Solid SOAP/REST Workday API skills; XML/JSON; XSLT or similar; OAuth 2.0.
  • RaaS (advanced reports exposed as web services).
  • Workday security concepts: ISU, ISSG (constrained/unconstrained), domain security policies.
  • Version control and CI/CD for integration code; test planning and execution.
  • Clear documentation and stakeholder communication.
  • Day‑to‑day tech:
    • Workday: EIB, Studio, Core/Cloud Connectors, PECI, SOAP/REST, RaaS.
    • Formats/transport: XML, JSON, CSV, SFTP/HTTPS, PGP.
    • Tools: Git, JIRA, Confluence, Postman.
Our hiring process:

  • TA Call: Meet one of our Talent Experts and get to know Atolls better.
  • Technical Round: Focus on the technical aspects of the role (Through a Live Case), and meet your potential manager.
  • Final Round: Meet other Atollians 🏝️ It varies from 1 to 3 interviews
  • Some processes might slightly change according to needs


At Atolls, we embrace diversity and inclusivity. Your unique background and perspectives will contribute to our shared success. If some skills are new to you but you’re eager to learn, we’d still love to hear from you!

Our Benefits:

At Atolls, we believe in nurturing both your professional and personal growth. Here’s what you can expect:

  • A culture that values personal and professional development, with internal mobility opportunities.
  • A supportive and open-minded team that embraces diverse perspectives and innovative ideas.
  • 32 days of paid vacation plus your birthday off, giving you the time you need to recharge.
  • A flexible hybrid working scheme to balance work and life.
  • Access to a learning budget and internal training to help you grow in your role.
  • Mental health coaching to support your well-being.
  • Regular global and local get-togethers to celebrate successes and build connections.
  • The possibility of taking a sabbatical after two years with the company.
  • A cloud-based company setup, providing flexibility and collaboration opportunities no matter where you are.
  • These are global benefits that apply to all employees, with additional local perks based on your location.


Are You Up for It?

Heads up! To make our process smoother, please apply to a maximum of three roles that best match your fit. This helps us focus on finding the right opportunity for you. Thanks!

We’re looking forward to receiving your CV in English! If relevant, please include any supporting documents or links to relevant social media profiles.

At Atolls, we want to ensure that all employees can thrive in an inclusive environment. Our employment opportunities are open to every gender, race, religion, age, sexual orientation, ability, place of origin, or socioeconomic status. We remain committed to a culture of diversity, equity and belonging, where all employees are welcomed, respected, connected, and engaged.

Expect hands‑on work with Workday EIB, Studio, Core/Cloud Connectors, PECI, SOAP/REST APIs, and RaaS.Responsibilities:In this role, you will:Design, build, test, and deploy Workday integrations (inbound/outbound) using EIB, Studio, and Core/Cloud Connectors.Develop/ maintain and support payroll interfaces.Build API integrations using Workday SOAP and REST services; implement OAuth 2.0 for REST and credentials for SOAP as appropriate.Deliver RaaS (Reports‑as‑a‑Service) integrations for bulk/reporting data needs.Set up secure transports (SFTP/HTTPS), PGP encryption, and certificate management.Model and map data for HCM, Absence, Compensation & Benefits, and Finance objects; handle deltas and effectively manage data.Define and manage Workday Integration System Users (ISU) and Integration System Security Groups (ISSG) with least‑privilege domain access.Write functional and technical specs; create mapping docs and sequence diagrams.Plan and run unit, SIT, and UAT; promote to prod using change control.Monitor integrations, build dashboards/alerts, and handle incident response with root‑cause fixes.Partner with the Product Manager on requirements and roadmap.Your Profile:4+ years in Workday integrations, with production ownership.Strong with EIB, Studio, and Core/Cloud Connectors. Hands‑on with payroll interfaces.Solid SOAP/REST Workday API skills; XML/JSON; XSLT or similar; OAuth 2.0.RaaS (advanced reports exposed as web services).Workday security concepts: ISU, ISSG (constrained/unconstrained), domain security policies.Version control and CI/CD for integration code; test planning and execution.Clear documentation and stakeholder communication.Day‑to‑day tech:Workday: EIB, Studio, Core/Cloud Connectors, PECI, SOAP/REST, RaaS.Formats/transport: XML, JSON, CSV, SFTP/HTTPS, PGP.Tools: Git, JIRA, Confluence, Postman.Our hiring process:TA Call: Meet one of our Talent Experts and get to know Atolls better.Technical Round: Focus on the technical aspects of the role (Through a Live Case), and meet your potential manager.Final Round: Meet other Atollians 🏝️ It varies from 1 to 3 interviewsSome processes might slightly change according to needsAt Atolls, we embrace diversity and inclusivity. Your unique background and perspectives will contribute to our shared success. If some skills are new to you but you’re eager to learn, we’d still love to hear from you!Our Benefits:At Atolls, we believe in nurturing both your professional and personal growth. Here’s what you can expect: A culture that values personal and professional development, with internal mobility opportunities. A supportive and open-minded team that embraces diverse perspectives and innovative ideas. 32 days of paid vacation plus your birthday off, giving you the time you need to recharge. A flexible hybrid working scheme to balance work and life. Access to a learning budget and internal training to help you grow in your role. Mental health coaching to support your well-being. Regular global and local get-togethers to celebrate successes and build connections. The possibility of taking a sabbatical after two years with the company. A cloud-based company setup, providing flexibility and collaboration opportunities no matter where you are.These are global benefits that apply to all employees, with additional local perks based on your location.Are You Up for It?Heads up! companyDescription: Keyrock is a global crypto investment firm offering industry-leading solutions in Market making, OTC, and Options for digital assets.
companySlogan: Changing the course of finance
descriptionHtml:
About Keyrock

Since our beginnings in 2017, we've grown to be a leading change-maker in the digital asset space, renowned for our partnerships and innovation.

Today, we rock with over 180 team members around the world. Our diverse team hails from 42 nationalities, with backgrounds ranging from DeFi natives to PhDs. Predominantly remote, we have hubs in London, Brussels, Singapore and Paris, and host regular online and offline hangouts to keep the crew tight.

We are trading on more than 80 exchanges, and working with a wide array of asset issuers. As a well-established market maker, our distinctive expertise led us to expand rapidly. Today, our services span market making, options trading, high-frequency trading, OTC, and DeFi trading desks.

But we’re more than a service provider. We’re an initiator. We're pioneers in adopting the Rust Development language for our algorithmic trading, and champions of its use in the industry. We support the growth of Web3 startups through our Accelerator Program. We upgrade ecosystems by injecting liquidity into promising DeFi, RWA, and NFT protocols. And we push the industry's progress with our research and governance initiatives.

At Keyrock, we're not just envisioning the future of digital assets. We're actively building it.

Mission statement

We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled Automation Engineer to lead the development and maintenance of automation systems that validate our critical business processes and drive measurable quality across the organisation. The ideal candidate will play a key role in building automated test coverage, performance monitoring, and deployment validation pipelines, while also ensuring the broader QA framework is effectively adopted across teams. The candidate will be focused on building out these automated testing frameworks for cryptocurrency market making technology, with inherent high availability and quality standards. Familiarity with similar systems will be helpful.

This role is highly cross-functional: you will collaborate with product, engineering, and delivery to implement best practices that support scalable, reliable systems.

Job Description

Key Responsibilities:

You will report to a Delivery Manager, and be guided by a QA Capability Lead to align practices across the business:

  • Design, implement, and maintain automation frameworks for validating end-to-end business processes and critical system functions.
  • Develop test strategies that support key QA framework KPIs, including coverage of critical business logic, process adherence, change failure rates, and performance monitoring.
  • Build and integrate automated testing into CI/CD pipelines to enable fast, safe releases.
  • Partner with Product and Delivery Managers to drive adoption of standard QA processes across teams.
  • Support the creation and maintenance of high-quality documentation for critical business functions and features.
  • Implement monitoring and alerting to ensure production stability and fast incident response.
  • Analyse trends in deployment success/failures and performance degradations to proactively improve system quality.
  • Track and report progress on quality metrics and KPIs to stakeholders.
  • Contribute to operational runbooks for incident response and system recovery.
  • Mentor engineers and QA team members on automation and testing best practices.

Background And Experience

  • Proven experience designing and maintaining automation frameworks (backend and functional).
  • Proficiency with modern automation tools (e.g., Playwright, Cypress, Python, JavaScript/TypeScript).
  • Strong understanding of QA frameworks and standard processes (process adherence, test coverage, KPI-driven quality tracking).
  • Experience with CI/CD pipelines and automated quality gates.
  • Understanding & monitoring of unit testing coverage.
  • Familiarity with API testing and backend validation (REST, gRPC).
  • Strong ability to link tests to business-critical functionality and risk-based coverage.
  • Solid knowledge of performance monitoring and alerting tools (e.g., Datadog, Grafana) and production health monitoring.
  • Strong experience with version control (Git) and issue tracking (Jira, Shortcut).
  • Incident management and root cause analysis (Change Failure Rate, MTTR).
  • Excellent collaboration and communication skills with cross-functional teams.
  • Ability to drive documentation and traceability efforts for critical functionality.
  • Familiarity with test management tools (Testmo, TestRail) and QA reporting.
  • Business analysis skills to support identifying critical business logic.
  • Self-starter with excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
  • Familiarity with UI testing and web browser automation
  • An interest in the cryptocurrency industry and market making is a plus.

Our recruitment philosophy

We value self-awareness and powerful communication skills in our recruitment process. We seek fiercely passionate people who understand themselves and their career goals. We're after those with the right skills and a conscious choice to join our field. The perfect fit? A crypto enthusiast who’s driven, collaborative, acts with ownership and delivers solid, scalable outcomes.

Our offer

  • A competitive salary package
  • Autonomy in your time management thanks to flexible working hours and the opportunity to work remotely
  • The freedom to create your own entrepreneurial experience by being part of a team of people in search of excellence

As an employer we are committed to building a positive and collaborative work environment. We welcome employees of all backgrounds, and hire, reward and promote entirely based on merit and performance.

Due to the nature of our business and external requirements, we perform background checks on all potential employees, passing which is a prerequisite to join Keyrock.

https://keyrock.com/careers/

With over 20,000 clients across 90+ countries—we’re the largest in our industry and shaping the future of travel, together. 

We're always seeking bright new talent to join the FareHarbor family (that's your cue)! Find out more at: https://fareharbor.com/jobs/
companySlogan: FareHarbor fuels the experiences of the travel industry by providing tour operators with simple online booking solutions
descriptionHtml:
About FareHarbor

At FareHarbor, our mission is to make experiences better for everyone. Founded in 2013 in Hawaii and acquired by Booking Holdings in 2018, FareHarbor creates powerful tools that enable our clients (think boat rentals, museums, food tours, events and more!) to operate and grow.

With over 20,000 clients across 90+ countries—we’re the largest in our industry and shaping the future of travel, together.

Our team is an ‘Ohana of 700+ people around the world. We’re passionate about pioneering an industry, embracing challenges with open arms, and delivering value to the experiences industry.

FareHarbor Core Values

  • Think Client First
  • We Are One ‘Ohana
  • Be Curious and Learn
  • Own It.
  • Act With Integrity
  • Embrace the Challenge

Why FareHarbor?

Founding FareHarbor required unwavering passion. Turning a start-up into the world’s leading and largest reservation software for tours, activities, and attractions required relentless dedication and vision. To date, we’ve helped over 20,000 global businesses operate successfully and are proud to have played a role in enabling business owners to live their dreams.

And since day one, we’ve known that our real success lies in our people—the Ohana.

With each new feature launched and new client onboarded, there is a team of incredible people behind the scenes who are full of dedication, passion, energy, and the will to succeed. We encourage everyone to bring their whole selves to work—to believe in their abilities, to freely express their creativity, and to contribute with their own uniqueness by wearing their true colors. We take care of one another and always prioritize health and wellbeing. We give our people the space and trust to learn, to try, to succeed, to collaborate, to think outside of the box, to make mistakes, and even to fail. And then we come together to try again.

From the minute you join, you have a voice. You find your space. You make an impact. We celebrate our victories, shout our successes, and are always eager to tackle new challenges. And we can’t wait to see all that’s to come.

What You’ll Do Here

FareHarbor, a Booking holding Company, is looking for a Software Engineer to join our Platform Engineering team in Amsterdam. This role focuses on solving shared challenges across Product Teams, delivering reusable tools/components, and improving developer productivity, with scalability and security best practices as a key consideration.

Our platform supports tens of thousands of businesses daily, and our external APIs integrate with major partners such as Viator, TripAdvisor, and VELTRA, helping our clients expand their reach. We are looking for a software engineer first and foremost, but one who also has a strong understanding of scalability, distributed systems, performance and security fundamentals to help ensure that our systems remain high performing, secure and resilient.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design and implement complex backend systems with a strong focus on performance, scalability and security.
  • Build reliable, maintainable, and well-documented infrastructure and tooling that empowers internal developers and ensures high system availability.
  • Contribute to the evolution of our platform architecture, identifying areas to improve developer experience, system performance, and code maintainability.
  • Write clean, well-tested, production-quality code and reusable libraries that enhance engineering velocity across the organization.
  • Collaborate closely with security, infrastructure, and product engineering teams to identify and mitigate risks while balancing performance and usability.
  • Proactively drive initiatives around observability, performance tuning, API optimization, and CI/CD improvements.
  • Support incident response, root cause analysis, and postmortems to continually improve system robustness and reliability.
  • Advocate for best practices in system design, performance-aware development, and secure coding across engineering teams.
  • Mentor and support other engineers in adopting scalable and maintainable development patterns.

Requirement

  • 6+ years of experience working with large-scale, production systems and mission-critical codebases.
  • Proficient in Python with strong experience in Django or similar frameworks.
  • Demonstrated expertise in addressing performance bottlenecks, improving scalability, and optimizing developer workflows.
  • Familiarity with secure coding principles and ability to design APIs and services that are both performant and secure.
  • Experience designing and implementing RESTful APIs and modern authentication mechanisms (OAuth, JWT, etc.).
  • Solid understanding of CI/CD pipelines, automated testing strategies, and release engineering.
  • Comfortable working across the stack and collaborating across teams in a fast-paced, agile environment.
  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical ideas clearly.
  • Bonus: hands-on experience with platform security practices or operational security (e.g., Red/Blue/Purple team work).

Benefits

  • Global leave benefit
    • 22 weeks paid parental leave
    • 2 weeks paid grandparent leave
    • Extended care and bereavement leave
    • Life insurance policy
  • Pension Plan
  • Central Amsterdam Location
  • Discount CZ insurance
  • Working in a multicultural environment - 45 different nationalities
  • Commuting allowance for public transport & subsidized lunch
  • Wellness benefits (Headspace subscription & wellness webinars)
  • Hybrid friendly
  • Work-from-home assistance
  • Educational Opportunities
  • Social hours & events and team-building
  • 26 vacation days per year
FareHarbor is committed to creating a diverse environment, and we are an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, or veteran status. We welcome talent that can offer us new insights and perspectives on challenges that we face, and we take measures to eliminate unconscious bias throughout the interview and hiring process. In tandem, we work to cultivate an inclusive culture in which all of our employees can be their authentic selves.

To learn more about how we use your information, see our Privacy Statement for Applicants. By submitting your application, you confirm that you understand and agree that your information will be processed in accordance with our Privacy Statement for Applicants.

Any offer of work (e.g. employment, assignment) will be subjected to the successful completion of pre-employment screening.

descriptionHtml:
Job Description

  • Design and implement highly available, multi-cloud infrastructure
  • Build observability and automation solutions that prevent incidents, not just detect them
  • Lead technical initiatives across teams, mentoring engineers and driving SRE best practices
  • Own production reliability, from architecture decisions to incident response
  • Optimize performance across the full stack - from kernel tuning to application-level improvements

Required Qualifications

  • 7+ years of SRE/DevOps experience with large-scale distributed systems
  • Strong OpenStack experience (VIO, RHOSP, or similar distributions)
  • Expert-level automation skills with Ansible and Terraform
  • Strong programming skills in Python and Bash scripting
  • Experience with Git-based CI/CD pipelines (GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, or similar)
  • Deep understanding of networking (TCP/IP, BGP, load balancing, VPN technologies)
  • Experience building and maintaining custom orchestration solutions
  • Proven track record of improving system reliability and reducing operational overhead

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with multiple OpenStack distributions and deployment models
  • Kubernetes experience with managed services (GKE, EKS, AKS)
  • Experience with SASE, SD-WAN, or zero-trust architectures
  • Familiarity with FortiMonitor or similar monitoring platforms (Prometheus)
  • Security certifications or demonstrated security expertise

Technical Stack

Private Cloud: OpenStack (VIO, RHOSP) | Automation: Ansible, Terraform, Custom Orchestration Engine | Languages: Python, Bash | CI/CD: Git-based pipelines | Monitoring: FortiMonitor, Prometheus, Grafana | Public Cloud: Google Cloud (GKE), AWS (EKS), Azure | Fortinet Products: FortiGate, FortiAnalyzer, EMS

companyDescription: Accenture is a leading global professional services company that helps the world’s leading businesses, governments and other organizations build their digital core, optimize their operations, accelerate revenue growth and enhance citizen services—creating tangible value at speed and scale. We are a talent- and innovation-led company with approximately 791 000 people serving clients in more than 120 countries. Technology is at the core of change today, and we are one of the world’s leaders in helping drive that change, with strong ecosystem relationships. We combine our strength in technology and leadership in cloud, data and AI with unmatched industry experience, functional expertise and global delivery capability. Our broad range of services, solutions and assets across Strategy & Consulting, Technology, Operations, Industry X and Song, together with our culture of shared success and commitment to creating 360° value, enable us to help our clients reinvent and build trusted, lasting relationships. We measure our success by the 360° value we create for our clients, each other, our shareholders, partners and communities.
companySlogan: We deliver on the promise of technology and human ingenuity. Let there be change.
descriptionHtml:
The Work

As a Senior Azure Cloud Engineer in Accenture’s Managed Services team, you will be responsible for ensuring the stability, resilience, and optimization of client Azure cloud environments. You will manage incident resolution, break-fix activities, and change implementation, applying ITIL processes to deliver seamless service to our global clients and collaborate with our international team.

With deep technical expertise and strong problem-solving skills, you will act as a senior escalation point for critical incidents, mentor junior engineers, and collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to drive operational excellence. This role requires not only technical proficiency but also effective communication and client-facing skills to ensure delivery of exceptionally managed services.

Key Responsibilities

As you step into this role, you will be expected to demonstrate hands-on expertise in deploying, configuring, and optimizing Azure resources to meet evolving business requirements. You will leverage industry-leading tools and frameworks to uphold the highest standards of operational efficiency and service continuity, seamlessly adapting to dynamic client landscapes. Your analytical acumen will ensure that all technical solutions are both resilient and scalable, anticipating future challenges before they arise. A keen attention to detail, coupled with a passion for learning, will enable you to stay ahead of technological advancements and emerging cloud trends.


  • Break-Fix & Change Execution: Conduct root-cause analysis, post-mortems, remediate issues, and implement changes following ITIL guidelines.
  • Client Engagement: Act as a trusted advisor and escalation point for clients on Azure-related incidents, service requests, and operational improvements.
  • Operations & Monitoring: Ensure proactive monitoring, performance tuning, and capacity management for Azure infrastructure and services.
  • Governance & Compliance: Adhere to ITIL standards, security best practices, and client governance frameworks.
  • Collaboration & Knowledge Sharing: Work closely with global delivery teams, service managers, and architects to implement operational improvements and knowledge transfer.
  • Continuous Improvement: Identify opportunities for automation and efficiency in day-to-day operations using tools like Zabbix or terraform, github, power automation, Ansible
  • You will also be expected to remain agile in the face of shifting business priorities, adapting quickly to new projects and emerging technologies within the Azure ecosystem. Proactivity is key—you will regularly review and enhance cloud environments to optimize cost and performance, and you will champion the adoption of automation and infrastructure-as-code methodologies wherever possible. Your role will be instrumental in shaping and refining incident response strategies, ensuring that lessons learned translate into improved resilience and operational maturity for our clients.


    • We’re looking for someone who enjoys working in dynamic environments, takes initiative, and is eager to keep learning and growing. In this role, you’ll have the opportunity to make a real impact—both for our clients and across our global Accenture community—by helping to shape the future of cloud managed services.


  • Mentorship: Guide and support junior engineers, sharing expertise and promoting a culture of excellence.
  • People: You will collaborate with cross-functional teams, including system, administrators, Service Managers, and project managers, to ensure seamless integration and operation of cloud services for our customers. Your ability to communicate effectively and mentor junior team members will be essential in fostering a collaborative and efficient working environment.
  • Technology: Your in-depth understanding of cloud architecture and Azure's capabilities will be instrumental in troubleshooting, managing, maintaining, migrating, and optimizing workloads for robust landing zones and other cloud infrastructure components for our customers to streamline project workflows and ensure the reliability and scalability of our customers' cloud environments.
  • With all our roles, there is some in-person time for collaboration, learning and building relationships with clients, peers, leaders, and communities. As an employer, we will be as flexible as possible to support your specific work/life needs.


    • Who We Are

    Through unmatched industry experience, leading technologies from our ecosystem partners and startups, and the largest delivery network in the world, we provide a powerful range of capabilities that can be tailored to our client’s most complex business needs. With over 100 innovation hubs deployed around the world, we help clients continuously innovate at speed and at scale so they can outpace their peers. You will bring innovation, intelligence, and industry experience together with the newest technologies to help clients innovate at scale and transform their businesses.

    Technology is one of five services that make up one Accenture – the others are Strategy & Consulting, Operations, Song, and Industry X.

    Here is what you will need:


  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field
  • Minimum of 5 years of relevant working experience with Microsoft Azure fundamentals and Associate Certified
  • Proven experience in Disaster recovery, migrating Azure projects
  • Strong understanding of cloud architecture and cloud security principles, (certifications are a plus)
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills (in Dutch and English)
  • Experience with scripting languages or programming skills, Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools such as Bicep or Terraform
  • A passion for automation: Powershell, Ansible, Infrastructure as a Code and other
  • Zabbix experience
  • Reverse proxying, secret management
  • GitOps methodology – preferably
  • API Management or API usage experience
  • experience with CI/CD and working in a DevOps environment
  • Azure Certified Azure Hands-on Fundamentals:(AI-900), Azure Fundamental (AZ-900), Azure Data Fundamental (DP-900)
  • Azure Associate: Azure Administration (AZ-104)


    • Bonus points:


  • Experience with Kubernetes for container orchestration
  • Proficiency in Python for scripting and automation
  • Knowledge of Generative AI (GenAI) and its applications
  • Understanding of data management and analytics in the cloud
  • Linux does not scare you, the world is bigger than Windows
  • Azure Associate: Data Scientist (DP-100), Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst, (PL300), Azure AI Engineer (AI-102)
  • Microsoft Certified Expert (AI-102, Azure Solutions Architect (AZ-305)
  • VMware Cloud Director (VCD), VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF-9.0)
  • Kubernetes


    • Qualifications & Experience

    Required:


  • Minimum 5 years of hands-on experience in IT operations, troubleshooting, and managed services support
  • Strong technical expertise in Microsoft Azure Cloud (infrastructure, networking, identity, monitoring, and security)
  • Microsoft Azure Cloud Foundation certification (or higher-level Azure certifications)
  • ITIL Foundation certification with proven application of ITIL processes (Incident, Problem, Change).
  • Experience with incident resolution, break-fix, and change implementation in enterprise-scale environments.
  • Strong stakeholder and communication skills, able to explain technical issues in business-friendly terms.


    • Preferred:


  • Experience in a Managed Services Provider (MSP) or global delivery environment
  • Knowledge of automation and scripting (PowerShell, Bicep, Terraform, etc.)
  • Exposure to hybrid cloud environments integrating on-premises and Azure services
  • Advanced Azure certifications (e.g., AZ-104 Administrator, AZ-305 Solutions Architect, AZ-700 Network Engineer).


    • Key Competencies


  • Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking
  • Ability to manage multiple incidents and priorities under pressure
  • Customer-centric mindset with a commitment to service excellence
  • Effective collaboration in a global, multicultural, and virtual team environment
  • Proactive in continuous learning and keeping up with evolving cloud technologies.


    • What’s in it for you?

    Accenture is a global company with a world-class brand and reputation. Our people are Change Makers, and to support them as an employer, we make five promises:


  • Work with flexibility, in a culture based on trust
  • Embrace a sustainable growth mindset
  • Live a human-led culture of Inclusion & Diversity by caring deeply for all our people.
  • Lead and transform our clients with technology
  • Connect with our global network of 700.000 colleagues.


    • These promises are reflected through our excellent employee benefits, including:


  • 26 vacation days per year
  • Paid transport (budget for electric bicycle or car, NS-Business card)
  • A laptop, iPhone, and home office setup allowance
  • Pension scheme, Accenture shares, annual bonus, collective health insurance scheme
  • Unlimited learning: access to thousands of free courses, classes and workshops.


    • What We Believe

    Accenture is an equal opportunity employer. Our leaders are committed to building a better, stronger and more equitable company for future generations to create positive, long-lasting change. Inclusion and diversity are fundamental to our culture and core values. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Our rich diversity makes us more innovative and creative, which helps us better serve our clients and our communities.

    Our position as partner to many of the world’s leading businesses, organizations and governments affords us both an extraordinary opportunity and a tremendous responsibility to make a difference. Sustainability is one of our greatest responsibilities, which we embed it into everything we do and for everyone we work with.

    Accenture is committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all. Please let us know if you require reasonable accommodation to enable your participation in the recruitment process by emailing stephanie.vogt@accenture.com. We will be happy to assist you.

    Please send us your CV in English.
